“If that protects you and our children…” the alpha continued, ignoring her own pain as she spoke, “I’ll leave everything behind for you.”

That hit Agatha brutally, because she knew the weight of that sentence. She knew what it meant for Rio Vidal to abandon everything.

Her mother, her sisters, the few people she loved, the place where she built every piece of her own identity while trying to survive the family she had.

Tears immediately slipped down Agatha’s face.

“No…” she whispered, shaking her head. “No… I don’t want you to do that.”

Rio immediately moved closer, as if she couldn’t stand seeing her cry. She held Agatha’s face carefully between her hands, her thumbs wiping away the warm tears running down her cheeks.

“Look at me…” she asked softly.

Agatha obeyed through tears, and she saw Rio was close to breaking too, but unlike other times, she wasn’t trying to run away.

Or hide.

“If nothing works… my decision is made,” Rio said firmly, even though her voice trembled slightly. “You are my priority.”

Her hand slowly slid down to Agatha’s stomach again.

“The three of you are.”

Agatha closed her eyes for a second as another tear escaped, because nobody had ever put her first like that before.

Rio slowly rested her forehead against hers.

“I spent my entire life living the way they told me to,” she murmured. “Being exactly what they expected from me… a perfect Vidal, obedient, useful… and empty.”

Her fingers slid across Agatha’s face with painful tenderness.

“But I can’t go back to that anymore after you.”

Agatha let out a quiet sob. Rio pressed her lips against hers in a slow kiss. When she pulled away, she stayed close enough to breathe the same air.

Agatha still cried softly, and Rio hated that. Hated seeing any trace of fear in her.

The alpha then took Agatha’s hand and brought it to her lips, kissing her fingers slowly before closing her eyes for a moment.

“So listen carefully…” she said softly, her voice rough with emotion. “If I have to spend the rest of my life facing or obeying every move that woman makes… then so be it. I’ll do it.”

Agatha felt another tear escape. Rio opened her eyes again.

“Because I’m going to protect you,” she murmured, sliding her hand to Agatha’s stomach again. “And I’m going to protect our pups.”

The word our hit Agatha directly in the chest.

Rio inhaled deeply, leaning closer until she partially hid her face against Agatha’s neck for a few seconds.

“I don’t care about the company… the family name… money… any of it,” she confessed quietly. “Not when I compare it to you.”

Agatha lifted a hand to her hair, slowly stroking the dark strands still slightly damp from the rain. Rio immediately closed her eyes at the touch.

“You’re not going through this alone either,” Agatha whispered through tears. “I’m with you.”

Rio let out a shaky breath, then lifted her face again and kissed Agatha intensely this time.

◆

The rest of the afternoon passed at a strange pace, far too calm after the chaos of that morning. Agatha tried convincing herself that was a good thing.

The last furniture pieces arrived near the beginning of the evening: the white dresser she had bought for the babies’ room, her office desk still disassembled inside the boxes, and a few smaller pieces missing from the living room since the move.

Little by little, the apartment was finally starting to look truly livable.

Rio silently helped the delivery men carry everything inside, completely ignoring the fact that she clearly didn’t need to physically handle any of it. Even so, she carried boxes as if she needed to prove something.

Agatha watched everything from the couch while resting her swollen legs a little, one hand resting over her already heavy belly.

The contrast still felt absurd to her — Rio Vidal, heir to one of the largest companies in the world, carrying disassembled furniture into a simple apartment while arguing with a delivery man about not hitting the freshly painted wall with the dresser.

That made Agatha smile without realizing it.

When the men finally left and silence returned to the apartment, Rio let out a tired breath while closing the door behind them, Agatha sank deeper into the couch.

“I think I officially moved in now…” she murmured, looking at the boxes scattered around the room.

Rio walked toward her while loosening the cuffs of her dress shirt rolled to her elbows. Her dark hair was slightly messy from the effort, and she looked absurdly beautiful in that more casual state.

“There’s still stuff missing,” she commented while looking around.

“The cribs arrive next week.”

That immediately caught Rio’s attention. She turned toward Agatha far too quickly.

“Cribs?”

Agatha held back a laugh.

“Yes… babies usually use those.”

Rio discreetly rolled her eyes before moving closer to the couch.

“I’ll build them.”

Agatha arched an eyebrow.

“Rio… I hired someone for that.”

“No need,” she replied immediately, offended by the idea. “I definitely want to build our pups’ first crib.”

The possessive way she said it made something warm spread through Agatha’s chest, and she ended up laughing softly.

“Alright then, controlling lady.”

Rio gave a small satisfied smile before sitting beside her on the couch, finally resting.

Agatha silently watched her profile before asking:

“Do you want to eat something? I can order something.”

Rio turned to answer, but the phone started ringing first.

The name on the screen immediately changed something in her. Agatha noticed instantly. It was subtle, but terrifying, as if an invisible mask had slid back over Rio’s face.

Rio sighed softly before answering.

“Hello?”

Agatha watched silently as her expression became increasingly tense, her eyes darkening and her breathing changing. Rio slowly ran a hand over her face before finally answering:

“…I’m already on my way.”

Then she hung up.

The silence immediately became heavy. Agatha felt her stomach tighten.

“What happened?” she asked, already serious. “Did your grandmother do something?”

Rio shook her head.

“No…” she answered quietly. “Actually, it’s my mother.”

Agatha blinked slowly. She vaguely remembered Rio’s mother from the trip — far too quiet, far too beautiful, and far too sad, like someone permanently drugged by pain.

“What happened?” she asked again, more careful now.

Rio looked away for a moment, as if she truly didn’t want to drag her into this.

“I… don’t want to involve you in this.”

Agatha immediately rubbed her belly before answering:

“I think I’m involved enough already.”

That pulled a small exhausted sigh from Rio. The alpha nodded slowly, standing from the couch while grabbing the car keys from the kitchen counter.

“My mother’s an alcoholic… since I was young,” she explained while putting her blazer back on. “She’s been in and out of rehab since before I became an adult.”

Agatha stayed silent, listening. Rio rarely spoke about her family like this.

“She only managed to stop during my younger sisters’ pregnancy… they’re twins,” she continued. “And I think during mine too.”

The last sentence came out bitter.

Rio inhaled deeply before adding:

“She apparently destroyed part of a hotel bar today because they refused to serve her more alcohol.”

Agatha immediately felt her chest tighten imagining Rio receiving that kind of phone call her entire life, always putting out fires or fixing other people’s disasters.

Agatha slowly stood from the couch, grabbing her coat, and Rio immediately turned toward her.

“Agatha…”

“Let’s go,” she said simply.

“You don’t need to—”

“And you’re not going alone.”

The omega said it decisively, grabbing her purse before Rio could insist again that she stay.

Rio only watched her for a second, tired.

Then she silently nodded.

The two left the apartment without speaking much. The hallway still smelled like fresh paint and rain coming through the building’s open windows.

The elevator took forever to arrive, and Agatha noticed Rio gripping the car keys too tightly between her fingers.

The parking lot was still damp from the previous storm. The lights reflected across the wet asphalt while Rio unlocked the car remotely, immediately opening the passenger door for Agatha first.

Agatha got in slowly, adjusting her belly before pulling the seatbelt across herself. Rio carefully closed the door and walked around the car before getting in too.

The silence inside the vehicle felt heavy, Rio started the car and drove through Manhattan’s wet streets without saying anything.

She simply kept her eyes fixed on the road while her fingers lightly tapped against the steering wheel, Jaw locked. Shoulders tense. Breathing shallow.

Then slowly, Agatha slid her hand onto Rio’s thigh.

The alpha inhaled deeply immediately. Her fingers left the steering wheel for just a second to carefully caress Agatha’s hand before quickly intertwining their fingers together, as if she needed that to keep functioning.

About twenty minutes later, the car entered the illuminated area of the The Plaza Hotel.

Agatha immediately noticed the police cars and ambulance parked near the main entrance, and her stomach tightened.

Rio parked far too quickly and shut off the car before turning toward her.

“Agatha, you don’t need to—”

But the omega had already opened the door.

Rio let out a tired breath before stepping out too.

The two quickly walked through the luxurious hotel entrance while employees pretended not to notice the commotion.

A police officer approached as soon as he saw Rio.

“Mrs. Vidal.”

Rio didn’t even slow down. She simply pulled a few folded bills from her wallet and discreetly placed them into the man’s hand.

“Here.”

The officer accepted them without even properly looking.

“You can go in.”

A strange discomfort crawled up Agatha’s spine at how natural it all was, as if money simply erased scandals.

And apparently, it did.

The hotel bar was partially destroyed. Broken glass scattered across the floor, a strong smell of alcohol, a flipped table near the counter, and tense employees walking back and forth.

And in the middle of everything—

Rio’s mother.

Carmen Vidal sat in a chair near the bar, part of her head wrapped in bandages while a doctor tried to examine her.

Even like that, it was obvious how much she resembled Rio.

The same dark eyes. The exact same elegant face, but broken. Far more broken than Rio’s. Consumed.

Agatha silently observed everything while a hotel manager immediately approached.

“Mrs. Vidal, your mother became extremely aggressive when we refused to serve her more alcohol. She started breaking glasses… bottles… practically attacked the entire bar—”

“Go fuck yourself,” Carmen muttered completely drunk without even properly lifting her head.

The manager pretended not to hear.

“Why is she injured?” Rio asked seriously.

“She slipped on the alcohol on the floor and hit her head against the edge of the counter.”

Rio slowly dragged a hand across her face, utterly exhausted.

“Thank you,” she answered with overly controlled calm. “I’ll pay for all the damages. Put every expense under Vidal Industries.”

The manager immediately looked relieved, of course he did. 

Agatha looked back at Carmen while the doctor tried changing the bandage on her head again. The woman shoved the professional away roughly.

“Get the fuck away from me.”

Rio lost her patience right there. She walked toward her mother far too quickly.

“Can you really not go one month without causing problems for me, Carmen?”

The entire bar fell silent, Carmen slowly lifted her eyes toward her daughter, completely intoxicated.

“Go fuck yourself… I didn’t call you.”

Rio let out a dry laugh.

“I’m your emergency contact. So yes, you did call me.”

That immediately hit Carmen. The woman looked away for a second, but Rio continued:

“Do you have any idea how ridiculous this has become? Police? Ambulance? An entire destroyed hotel because you can’t stay sober for a few hours?”

Agatha noticed it instantly, Rio was being cruel because she was hurt, and because this hurt her too.

Carmen merely groaned irritably before unsteadily standing up.

“Go to hell just like your grandmother.”

Then she stumbled toward the women’s restroom, Rio closed her eyes for a second, breathing deeply and trying not to explode.

Agatha slowly approached.

“I’ll go after her.” Rio immediately opened her eyes.

“Agatha, she needs to grow up—”

But the omega was already walking away.

Rio ended up staying behind near the destroyed bar while Agatha followed the silent hallway toward the women’s restroom. Agatha entered the bathroom slowly after grabbing a bottle of water from one of the forgotten trays in the hotel hallway.

The women’s restroom was absurdly luxurious,light marble covered the walls, golden mirrors reflected the soft lighting that contrasted far too much with the chaos that had happened minutes earlier in the bar.

Carmen was leaning against the sink, breathing heavily while washing her face over and over again. Her dark hair was messy, her makeup smeared, and the bandage on her head made everything look even sadder.

“Hey… how are you?” Agatha asked, approaching carefully.

Carmen slowly turned her face toward her.

Her eyes looked lost.

“Tired…” she murmured hoarsely. Then she let out a small bitter laugh. “I… think I’m… lost.”

Agatha opened the bottle of water and walked toward her.

“Drink.” she said gently, handing her the water. “Whenever I’m wasted one of these helps… well, when I still drank.”

Carmen let out a small nasal “hm” and took the bottle, slowly sitting down on the cushioned bench near the sinks. She drank practically half the bottle in one go before breathing deeply afterward.

“Thank you…”

Agatha merely nodded before saying:

“She didn’t mean it…”

“Rio.” Carmen laughed humorlessly. “She’s right about me.”

“No…” Agatha answered immediately. “She’s just tired.”

Carmen let out a small disbelieving laugh.

Agatha took a deep breath before speaking again:

“By the way… I don’t know if you remember me.”

Carmen squinted, trying to focus on her.

“The… secretary…” she murmured before widening her eyes slightly. “Wow… you’re pregnant.”

Agatha automatically looked down at her own stomach before smiling faintly.

“Yeah…”

“With Rio’s?” Carmen immediately asked, blinking several times as if her brain had finally connected everything.

Agatha bit her lip lightly, guilty.

“Yeah…”

Carmen froze. Completely froze, Then blinked again.

“Wait…” she looked from Agatha’s stomach back to her face. “Rio’s?”

Agatha nodded slowly, and then Carmen simply let out a disbelieving laugh, emotional and destroyed all at once.

“I’m going to be a grandmother?”

That made Agatha smile genuinely for the first time since arriving there, giving her a small smile.

“Yeah…”

Carmen lifted a hand to her mouth, her eyes instantly filling with tears.

“My God…” she whispered quietly. “Rio is going to have children…”

Her voice cracked at the end, as if that meant more than she could put into words.

She looked at Agatha again, differently now. Softer. More present.

“She panicked when they called me from here,” Carmen commented hoarsely. “Even angry at me… she always comes.”

Agatha lowered her eyes for a moment, because she knew Rio always came.

After a few minutes, Agatha took a deep breath and lightly touched Carmen’s arm.

“I think you should go to the hospital and get your head checked…” she said carefully. “It could be a concussion.”

Carmen let out a tired sigh, running a hand through her damp hair.

“Yeah… probably.”

Agatha nodded and helped her stand up slowly, Carmen seemed smaller now, Fragile and very different from the elegant and silent woman Agatha remembered seeing beside Rio during that trip.

The two walked out of the restroom together.

Outside, the bar was still partially destroyed. Employees cleaned up shards of glass while a few guests whispered discreetly.

The hotel manager was talking to Rio near the broken counter.

Rio looked completely serious. Cold again.

The social mask perfectly back in place.

She was saying something about bank transfers and damage costs when she saw Agatha walking out with Carmen.

Her gaze immediately went to the bandage on her mother’s head.

Carmen walked straight past Rio without saying absolutely anything. She didn’t even look at her.

She simply kept walking toward the hotel exit, slowly heading toward the car.

The manager noticed the strange atmosphere and cleared his throat.

“Well… thank you again, Mrs. Vidal.”

Rio merely nodded.

“Everything will be resolved today.”

The man quickly said goodbye and left.

The silence became heavy the moment they were alone.

Rio immediately turned toward Agatha.

“Are you okay?” she asked quietly.

Agatha nodded slightly.

“Yes… but I think we should take her to the hospital to check for a concussion.”

Rio inhaled deeply and nodded.

“Of course.”

Agatha started walking past her toward the exit, but Rio firmly grabbed her hand first.

“Wait…”

Agatha turned around.

Rio’s eyes looked tired now.

“I’m sorry for all of this,” she said quietly. “I didn’t want you to see… that.” Agatha held her gaze for a few seconds before lightly squeezing the hand Rio held.

“I’ve seen a lot of family members with alcohol problems, Rio… it’s okay.”

The alpha closed her eyes for a moment, as if that eased something inside her.

Then she slowly nodded, but Agatha inhaled deeply before continuing:

“I understand you’re angry… but you shouldn’t talk to your mother like that.”

Rio immediately looked away, like someone who knew exactly that.

“I know,” she murmured. “I go too far sometimes.”

She slowly released Agatha’s hand, dragging a tired hand over her face.

“But I also know a good part of this is my grandmother’s fault.”

“What do you mean?”

Agatha frowned slightly.

Rio let out a bitter laugh devoid of humor.

“Rita spent my mother’s entire life calling her a murderer,” she said quietly. “Because of my grandmother Claire’s death.”

Agatha went completely still, utterly horrified.

“My God… that’s horrible.”

Rio slowly nodded.

“My mother grew up hearing she killed her own mother by being born. Imagine what that does to someone’s mind after years.”

Her jaw tightened subtly.

“Rita always made sure Carmen felt guilty for existing. Then she acted as if she were the only person trying to ‘save’ her.”

Agatha felt a strange tightness in her chest because she was starting to understand more clearly the silent terror that woman caused throughout the entire family.

Rio took another deep breath before looking at Agatha again, more vulnerable now.

“Sometimes I think everyone in this family was slowly destroyed by her.”

◆

Some time later, Agatha sat outside Carmen’s hospital room, silently observing the quiet movement in the hallway.

The strong scent of antiseptic lingered in the air, mixed with the distant sounds of heart monitors and stretchers rolling across the pale floor.

Carmen’s room door was partially open, Agatha could see Rio inside.

The alpha sat beside her mother’s bed, her body leaning slightly forward while listening to something Carmen said quietly.

Unlike the coldness she showed the rest of the world, there was a painful gentleness in the way Rio adjusted the blanket over her mother’s legs.

Carmen looked exhausted. Small in that hospital bed, Rio then carefully held her mother’s face and kissed her forehead for a long moment.

A simple gesture , but intimate,deeply intimate.

Agatha looked away for a moment, feeling something tighten painfully inside her chest because despite everything, despite the anger and resentment, Rio still loved her mother.

And that was a strange feeling for Agatha.

The door opened a few seconds later, Rio stepped out of the room, breathing deeply while partially closing the door behind her.

When she found Agatha sitting in the hallway chair, her shoulders finally seemed to relax a little. She walked toward Agatha and sat beside her.

“The doctor said it wasn’t anything serious,” she commented quietly, rubbing her tired hands together. 

“They’re going to keep her under observation for a few hours because of the hit… but she’ll be okay.”

Agatha slowly let out the breath she’d been holding, relieved.

“Thank God…”

“And apparently she knows she’s going to be a grandmother now,” Rio murmured disbelievingly.

Agatha ended up smiling faintly.

“I might’ve told her…” Rio let out a quiet laugh through her nose and nodded.

“That was good.”

Rio then looked directly at Agatha and held her hand over the chair.

“Thank you for being here.” The omega squeezed her fingers back.

“Good luck,” she said softly. “Because I’m going to be around for all of it now.”

That pulled a genuine smile from Rio. Very small, but completely sincere.

The alpha then slowly slid her hand to Agatha’s stomach over the fabric of her coat.

“Hey, you two… how are you doing in there?” she murmured, far too affectionate for someone like her.

Agatha smiled tiredly.

“Kicking a lot, actually,” she commented quietly. “Now that they can hear everything, they get much more agitated.”

Rio kept her hand there for a few seconds, feeling movement immediately respond beneath her palm.

Her eyes softened instantly.

“I can barely wait for the next ultrasound…” she confessed in a whisper.

“Me too.”

Rio’s mother was discharged close to six in the morning.

The hospital was already beginning to slowly wake up, with nurses changing shifts and the hallways gradually becoming busier.

The bluish light of dawn crossed through the emergency wing’s enormous windows, making everything look cold and exhausted.

Agatha had fallen asleep sitting in the hallway chair, her body curled slightly sideways with one hand resting over her stomach while breathing deeply, completely defeated by the exhaustion of the night.

Rio stepped out of the elevator carrying two cups of coffee in her hands.

When she saw Agatha sleeping like that, something inside her immediately softened.

The alpha approached slowly, quietly, and lightly touched the omega’s knee.

“Agatha…” she called softly.

Agatha slowly opened her eyes, still disoriented for a few seconds.

She blinked several times before stretching carefully in the chair, letting out a tired little groan from the discomfort in her back.

Rio handed one of the cups to her.

“Decaf,” she explained. “And I told you that you should’ve gone home.”

Agatha took the coffee while still half asleep.

“Quiet.” she murmured before taking a sip.

Rio ended up letting out a small amused breath through her nose.

That was when Carmen appeared in the hallway already changed, sober, and clearly embarrassed.

Her hair was still slightly messy, and the discreet bandage at the side of her head made last night’s accident obvious.

“Good morning…” she said quietly.

Rio immediately stood up.

“Good morning, Mom… how are you feeling?”

Carmen sighed tiredly.

“Fine… I think,” she murmured, looking away for a moment before facing them again. “Thank you, girls… Rio… thank you.”

Rio merely nodded slowly, stepping closer to lightly touch her mother’s shoulder.

The simple gesture seemed to calm Carmen more than any words could.

“The driver already arrived,” Carmen commented after a few seconds. “And you two need to go rest too.”

Her eyes stopped on Agatha for a moment, softer now.

“Especially you.”

Agatha smiled faintly.

“I will.”

The three walked together toward the hospital exit in silence.

Outside, New York’s sky was still covered by pale clouds left behind from the dawn rain.

The car waited parked near the main entrance.

The driver opened the door for Carmen, who inhaled deeply before getting inside.

“Bye, girls,” she said more quietly this time.

“Bye, Mom,” Rio replied.

Agatha also waved lightly.

The car drove away a few seconds later, and Rio slowly exhaled while watching the vehicle disappear down the wet street.

Then she immediately turned toward Agatha, wrapping her arms around her waist.

“Now home, young lady,” she murmured close to her face. “You’ve disobeyed way too much today.”

Agatha smiled tiredly, letting her body relax against Rio’s while remaining there in the alpha’s arms, breathing deeply as the world finally seemed to slow down a little around them.

◆

The hours passed quickly inside the new apartment, the rain had finally disappeared, leaving only a golden late afternoon light crossing through the wide living room windows.

The smell of food filled the apartment while Agatha finished setting the lunch table with exaggerated concentration.

She aligned the silverware for the third time, adjusted the glasses, then slightly moved one of the plates before taking a deep breath.

“Do you think it looks okay?” she asked while looking at Rio.

Rio immediately smiled.

The alpha was finishing putting on her dark blazer near the open kitchen counter, her hair still slightly damp from the shower and loosely pushed back carelessly.

“It looks perfect, sweetheart.”

Agatha slowly exhaled, resting both hands against the edge of the table.

“I just want… things to be okay between me and them,” she confessed more quietly. “They’re my best friends, Rio…”

Rio’s expression softened instantly.

She walked toward Agatha unhurriedly, delicately holding her face before kissing her cheek.

“Then you’ll be okay,” she murmured. “Seriously… Alice and I have fought so many times I honestly lost count. And well… we’re still here.”

Agatha let out a weak little laugh through her nose, trying to believe that.

She was about to answer when the sound of the doorbell echoed through the apartment.

Both of them automatically looked toward the door.

“That must be them…”

Agatha took a deep breath.

The nervousness returned immediately.

She walked toward the entrance trying to mentally prepare herself for the meeting, but when she opened the door she found Lilia standing in the hallway holding two large boxes in her arms.

“This arrived for you,” she said calmly.

Agatha blinked when she recognized the brand printed across the boxes, Her eyes widened slightly.

“The cribs…” she murmured in surprise. “They arrived earlier than I expected.”

Rio appeared right behind her.

“I’ll take them.”

The alpha picked up the boxes effortlessly, as if they were far too light, carrying them into the apartment while Agatha closed the door. Lilia watched the scene with a small amused smile.

“It’s adorable when they do those alpha tricks.”

Agatha ended up laughing softly.

“Yeah…”

Lilia adjusted her purse on her shoulder.

“I need to go, I’m cooking,” she commented. “I’ll send you some apple dessert later.”

“Thank you, Lilia.”

The older omega nodded before walking down the building hallway. Agatha closed the door, taking another deep breath while trying to control the anxiety that seemed to live permanently inside her chest since the move.

When she turned around, she found Rio walking into the empty room beside theirs, carefully placing the crib boxes on the floor.

The room was still practically empty, but it was already beginning to carry life.

The pale walls reflected the soft late afternoon light while the newly installed curtains moved slightly with the breeze slipping through the half-open window. A few boxes still rested in the corner, waiting for organization, furniture, details, and a very near future.

Rio remained standing inside the empty room for a few more seconds, staring at the crib boxes before finally returning to Agatha.

She seemed different since the conversation with Rita, as if she were now in a constant state of alert.

Agatha noticed immediately when Rio approached her in the living room and held her face between her hands with far too much care for someone so large.

The kiss came slowly, lingering and possessive in the way Rio kept her close for a few extra seconds before resting her forehead against hers.

“I’ll come back right after the meeting,” she murmured quietly.

Agatha caressed her arm.

“You don’t need to worry about me.”

Rio let out a small disbelieving laugh through her nose.

“Impossible,” she replied immediately. “Especially because you clearly intend to climb furniture to hang pictures on the walls.”

Agatha tried to look offended.

“I’m not going to do anything stupid.”

Rio narrowed her eyes slowly.

“Promise?”

“Promise.”

The alpha still looked suspicious, but she ended up stealing another quick kiss before grabbing the car keys and leaving the apartment.

The silence left behind afterward felt strange.

But not bad.

Agatha took a deep breath, running a hand over her stomach through the short yellow dress that perfectly outlined her rounded belly.

Then she walked toward the bathroom, grabbing a lip gloss from the sink and absentmindedly applying it while staring at her own reflection.

She still looked tired.

But happy.

And maybe for the first time in a long time.

Minutes later, the sound of the doorbell echoed through the apartment.

Agatha immediately felt her stomach tighten.

She knew who it was.

She took one deep breath before walking toward the door.

When she opened it, she found Wanda and Jen standing in the hallway.

Jen held a casserole dish covered with a patterned kitchen towel, clearly not knowing what to do with her own hands. Wanda remained completely silent beside her, arms crossed and wearing an expression far too serious.

The three of them spent several seconds just staring at one another.

Awkward.

Pathetic.

Far too human.

“Come in,” Agatha said softly, stepping aside.

The two slowly entered, observing the new apartment while walking through the living room. Jen carefully placed the casserole dish on the kitchen table as if that would somehow help with the absurd tension.

Then she walked back toward them, and nobody said anything for a few seconds until Agatha suddenly started crying out of nowhere.

The tears came instantly as she lifted a hand to her face.

“I’m sorry…” her voice came out tangled. “I just… the hormones… I…”

“Oh no,” Jen complained immediately, her own eyes already filling with tears too. “Don’t do this to me because I’m gonna start crying too.”

“Too late,” Wanda sniffled instantly.

That was enough.

The three of them ended up laughing and crying at the same time when Wanda pulled Agatha into a hug first, Jen immediately joining them afterward.

It turned into chaos of tangled arms, tears, and nervous laughter.

Agatha ended up hiding her face against their shoulders while crying uncontrollably.

“I love you guys so much…” she said through tears.

“We love you too, idiot,” Jen replied immediately, hugging her tighter.

“A lot,” Wanda added, kissing the top of Agatha’s head while crying too.

After all the crying, the three of them eventually ended up spread across Agatha’s new apartment living room.

Jen had served pieces of the casserole while Wanda opened sodas in the kitchen, and little by little the heavy atmosphere gave way to something lighter.

Agatha sat on the couch with her legs folded to the side and a blanket over her thighs while explaining everything that had happened over the past few days.

About Rio.

Her family.

The babies.

And especially Rita.

When she finished explaining that Rita had shown up at the apartment offering her “friendship,” Jen nearly choked.

“Is that bitch completely insane?” she asked in disbelief.

Agatha let out a humorless nasal laugh.

“Rita Vidal is probably one of the worst people I’ve ever met.”

Wanda remained more serious.

“And are you safe here?” she asked quietly.

Agatha inhaled deeply before nodding.

“For now, yes. Rita can’t do anything right now… but when the babies are born…” she lowered her eyes for a moment, absentmindedly caressing her belly. “I think eventually she’ll try something.”

Silence fell for a few seconds.

Jen immediately frowned.

“I’ll kill that old woman.”

“Jen,” Wanda automatically scolded.

“No, seriously.”

Agatha ended up smiling a little before continuing:

“Rio is already talking to lawyers… investigating family documents… trying to solve everything before the babies are born.”

Wanda rested her arms on her knees.

“And if she can’t?”

Agatha stayed silent for a moment before answering quietly:

“If nothing works… I’m leaving for Paris with her.”

The two of them immediately fell silent.

Wanda slowly exhaled.

“That woman is a monster…”

Agatha stretched her hand across the couch until she reached theirs, squeezing gently.

“She’s not going to win,” she said firmly despite the fear still existing inside her. “She’s not.”

Jen and Wanda slowly nodded.

Then Agatha decided to change the subject before she started crying again. She dramatically tossed a strand of hair behind her shoulder.

“So… did you already find someone to replace me?” she asked with a small arrogant smile. “Even though I’m obviously irreplaceable.”

Wanda laughed immediately.

“You were right. That apartment is chaos.”

“Infernal chaos,” Jen added.

Agatha arched an amused eyebrow.

“That bad?”

“Well…” Wanda tried to contain her smile. “Loki’s living with us now.”

Agatha immediately groaned.

“Oh, good luck.”

Jen rolled her eyes.

“We’re adapting… although the rotation of men in that bedroom is getting dangerously high.”

“Ew!” Agatha complained automatically.

The three of them ended up laughing together again afterward.

Wanda rested her chin against her hand while watching Agatha absentmindedly stir her soda.

“And where’s Rio anyway?” she asked.

Agatha immediately smiled faintly upon hearing her name.

It was involuntary at that point.

“She had to go to a meeting at the company… but she’s completely focused on the pregnancy now,” she commented while absentmindedly caressing her stomach. “I think she officially lost her mind. She even wants to build the cribs.”

Jen went completely motionless for two seconds.

“Rio Vidal… building cribs?”

Agatha shrugged with a small smile.

“Yep.”

“That sounds like a scene invented by a soap opera,” Jen murmured in disbelief.

Wanda started laughing.

“I would pay to see that.”

“Honestly?” Agatha smiled wider now. “Me too.”

Jen then seemed to remember something and quickly grabbed a large folder she had left beside her purse.

She dramatically held it out toward Agatha.

“Well… speaking of our nieces and nephews.”

Agatha raised an eyebrow.

“What is this?”

“Open it.”

Agatha slowly pulled open the folder flap and immediately found dozens of cutouts, color palettes, inspirations, and notes scattered between the pages.

There were images of blue and pink balloons, yellow and white ones, some purple, green, or gold, different themes, flowers, and sweets. At the bottom of the pages there were invitation and decoration ideas, even examples of baby shower tables.

Agatha’s eyes widened.

“My God, Jen…” she started laughing. “Don’t you know Pinterest exists?”

Jen dramatically tossed the hair of her lace wig behind her shoulder, pretending to be offended.

“I’m original, sweetheart.”

Wanda immediately rolled her eyes.

“She spent three nights doing this.”

“Four,” Jen corrected.

Agatha still flipped through the ideas completely stunned.

“What even is all this?” Wanda smiled more softly this time.

“Your baby shower, obviously. The ideas.” Agatha blinked in surprise.

Wanda continued:

“You need to have one. And now it’s not even out of necessity anymore, since you decided to choose the mother of your children directly from America’s richest list…”

Agatha snorted with laughter.

“Wanda!”

“But because we love you,” she finished more quietly now. “And we want to spoil your babies too.”

Agatha’s smile immediately softened into something more emotional.

“Okay…” she murmured while looking  the cutouts again. “I’ll talk to Rio.”

Jen slowly raised an eyebrow.

“You’re going to ask her permission?”

Agatha rolled her eyes instantly.

“Not even in her most erotic dreams,” she shot back immediately. “I’m just letting her know it’s happening.”

Wanda burst out laughing loudly, and Jen looked extremely satisfied with the answer, she then grabbed the tablet from the table and dramatically pointed toward the couch.

“Come on. We have a lot to discuss, mommy.”

Agatha laughed while slowly standing up.

“You guys are taking this way too seriously.”

“Of course we are,” Jen answered indignantly. “We need to decide on the theme, this is important.”

 

Chapter 14: The Baby Shower

 

┕──━──━─┑◆┍─━──━──┙

A few weeks had passed since Agatha’s move, and for the first time in a long while life seemed to have found some kind of strange balance between chaos, fear, and happiness.

The kitchen table in the apartment was completely covered with ribbons, papers, small decorations, and scattered invitations while Agatha, Wanda, and Jen finished the last details for the baby shower.

Of course Rio had loved the idea, and of course she had financed every single absurd idea Jen and Wanda came up with during the process without even blinking.

The chosen theme ended up being neutral, in soft shades of white and yellow, after a long aesthetic war involving Jen wanting something extravagant and Wanda begging for simplicity.

In the end, the rabbits won.

All because Agatha had started complaining daily that the babies “jumped” inside her belly like two hyperactive rabbits whenever Rio got close.

So the theme simply came together on its own.

Now little baby rabbits in a classic old-animation style decorated the invitations, party favors, and even the centerpieces spread around the room.

It was ridiculously adorable, and Rio seemed dangerously proud of it.

Agatha’s belly had gained considerable weight over the past few weeks.

The seventh month was officially beginning, and every warning light inside Rio Vidal’s brain had been permanently activated.

The alpha basically lived in a state of vigilance.

She wouldn’t let Agatha carry weight, climb on chairs, or even reach high cabinets.

She wouldn’t let her do absolutely anything.

The problem was that Agatha kept disobeying whenever Rio turned her back, which resulted in small, completely idiotic daily arguments.

Jen had even started counting how many times a day she heard:

“Agatha, get down from there.”

And honestly, it was a lot.

Rio had practically moved into the apartment for good now.

Her own apartment wasn’t adapted for children, and deep down she also didn’t want to take away Agatha’s comfort during this moment.

Nor did she want to put her through another move while pregnant with twins.

So Rio’s clothes slowly started appearing in the closets, her shoes near the front door, and her suits occupying half the wardrobe, and finally her books about alpha motherhood on the living room coffee table.

Even her woody scent seemed permanently soaked into the apartment now.

Agatha was finishing sealing one of the envelopes at the table when she felt a strong kick against the side of her belly.

She let out a tired little groan.

“Ow…”

Jen immediately looked up from the hot glue.

“What? Are you okay?”

“Your nephew just tried to escape through the left side of my stomach,” Agatha complained dramatically.

Wanda started laughing.

“That was definitely the boy.”

“You don’t even know if it’s a boy!”

“Yes, I do. He has the energy of someone who’s gonna be a problem.”

Wanda started laughing harder.

Agatha snorted a laugh while resting one hand on her belly. Another kick came right after, and she sighed.

“Rio spoiled them. They always go insane whenever she’s nearby.”

And as if she had been summoned by her own name, the sound of the electronic lock echoed through the apartment.

Jen immediately looked up.

“Speaking of the rich devil.”

The door opened seconds later and Rio walked in still wearing her dark work blazer, holding two huge shopping bags while speaking French on the phone with someone from the company.

Rio ended the call and kept looking through emails on her phone screen, but as soon as she approached the kitchen table all of her attention completely shifted direction.

She walked over to Agatha without even properly taking off her blazer, leaning down to affectionately kiss the top of her head.

“Everything ready, sweetheart?”

Agatha immediately smiled softly at the nickname.

“Yes, my love.”

She finished sealing the last invitation on the table.

The envelopes had turned out absurdly delicate, vintage-style, in white and yellow tones with small rabbits drawn in soft gold in the corners and the invitation carefully tucked inside, tied with a pale satin ribbon.

Rio took the envelope from Agatha’s hands just to observe it more closely.

Her expression instantly softened, becoming that look of someone who still didn’t entirely believe all of this was real.

The baby shower, the cribs, their children so close to arriving.

It was something that not even in a thousand years had existed in Rio’s imagination.

Agatha then tried to stand up from the chair by herself. Just tried, because Rio immediately grabbed her hands to help her.

“Rio,” Agatha complained. “I still know how to walk.”

Jen and Wanda rolled their eyes together at the exact same time.

“My God, you two are so disgustingly sweet,” Jen muttered dramatically.

“It’s becoming unbearable living near this,” Wanda added.

Rio completely ignored the two of them while helping Agatha stand up slowly, one of her hands immediately sliding protectively to her back.

Agatha simply smiled to herself, already used to it.

“Take care of my house,” Rio said while grabbing the car keys from the counter.

“You got it,” Wanda replied.

Jen pointed a wooden spoon in their direction.

“And bring the future grandma back in one piece.”

That pulled a soft laugh from Agatha because over the last few weeks Carmen really had been trying to get better.

After the last breakdown, she herself had decided to check into rehab again, and this time she genuinely seemed to be trying to stay sober.

Rio had been absurdly happy about it, Agatha could notice it easily.

She visited her mother whenever she could now, called her, sent food, and accompanied her to appointments.

And after a lot of insistence from Agatha, the two of them had decided to invite Carmen to the baby shower.

Rio opened the car door for Agatha as soon as they reached the building garage.

Agatha got in slowly, adjusting her belly before sighing comfortably into the seat.

Rio carefully closed the door behind her before walking around the car.

She got in on the driver’s side and started the engine, which purred softly.

Agatha adjusted her belly beneath the seatbelt while Rio slowly pulled out of the garage and into the still busy city traffic.

The omega rubbed her stomach absentmindedly in circular motions while watching the buildings pass by through the window.

Rio noticed almost immediately.

“Everything okay?” she asked, glancing quickly in her direction.

Agatha nodded.

“Yes…” she answered with a soft smile. “They just get excited when we’re in the car.”

That immediately made the corner of Rio’s mouth lift.

“So they’re spoiled already.”

“Blame their rich mother.”

Rio let out a soft laugh through her nose while making the turn toward the bridge.

Agatha then turned slightly toward her.

“And you? How are you?”

Rio stayed silent for a few seconds before answering honestly:

“A little anxious.”

Her hand tightened slightly around the steering wheel.

“But happy you’re coming with me.”

Agatha slowly slid her hand until she found Rio’s free hand resting on the gearshift, intertwining their fingers.

Rio immediately took a deep breath at the touch.

She kept driving with one hand while absentmindedly caressing Agatha’s fingers with her thumb.

The city slowly disappeared behind them.

The buildings gave way to emptier roads, wooded areas, and distant neighborhoods until they finally crossed the bridge toward Long Island.

The clinic where Carmen was staying was completely removed from the chaos of New York.

It was exclusive and quiet.

Hidden from the world.

When the car entered the long road surrounded by tall trees, Agatha immediately noticed the change in atmosphere.

Everything felt quieter there.

The main entrance was surrounded by dense forests and small luxurious cabins scattered throughout the property where the patients lived during treatment.

Some people walked alongside therapists through the gardens, others participated in outdoor activities near a lake in the distance.

It looked nothing like a normal clinic.

More like a retreat isolated from the rest of the world.

Rio’s car calmly drove up the small main hill before entering the large pale stone courtyard at reception.

She parked slowly and turned off the engine.

Agatha immediately turned toward her.

“Are you ready?”

Rio spent a few seconds staring ahead through the windshield.

Then she took a deep breath and nodded slowly.

“Yes.”

The two got out of the car slowly, and immediately Rio walked around the vehicle to help Agatha.

Agatha held the delicate invitation between her fingers while Rio grabbed her purse before the omega could even protest.

“You’ve become impossible,” Agatha murmured, amused.

“Correction,” Rio replied calmly while placing a hand on her back to guide her.

“I became responsible.”

Agatha rolled her eyes, but smiled anyway, the two walked together through the clinic’s main entrance.

The inside was just as luxurious as the rest of the property.

The reception area had pale marble floors, elegant sofas spread throughout the room, and enormous glass windows that let in the soft late-morning light along with the view of the surrounding forests.

Rio leaned against the counter while quietly giving her mother’s name to the receptionist.

Agatha took the opportunity to observe the place.

Some patients walked calmly through the hallways while others read near the lit fireplace in the main lounge.

She heard the receptionist make a quick call before smiling politely.

“You may follow me, please.”

Rio nodded and immediately returned to Agatha’s side.

The two followed the employee outside the main clinic, walking along a small stone path surrounded by tall trees until they reached one of the more isolated cabins on the property.

The receptionist knocked lightly before opening the door.

“Carmen? Your visitors are here.” Carmen immediately turned around.

The smile on her face appeared so quickly it was hard to believe this was the same destroyed woman Agatha had found at the hotel weeks ago.

She dropped the paintbrush onto the table beside her.

The entire cabin was filled with painting materials, canvases leaning against the walls, jars of paint, scattered brushes, and unfinished paintings.

Some paintings portrayed landscapes, others people and some were too abstract for Agatha to fully understand, but they were beautiful, very beautiful.

Agatha was genuinely impressed.

“Girls… I’m so glad you came,” Carmen said while walking toward them.

Rio approached first, affectionately kissing her mother’s cheek.

“Good morning.”

Carmen immediately smiled before opening her arms to Agatha.

“Hello, darling.”

Agatha carefully returned the hug because of her belly, and Carmen immediately lowered her eyes to it, completely enchanted.

“May I?”

Agatha smiled softly.

“Of course.”

Carmen gently rested her hands over the rounded belly and almost instantly received a small movement in response.

Her eyes widened slightly.

“They’re active.” Agatha let out a quiet little laugh.

“They always are when we travel by car.”

“Smart,” Carmen murmured while smiling at the belly as if speaking directly to the babies.

She then pointed toward the sofa near the windows.

“Please, sit.”

The two settled down while Carmen absentmindedly organized some paint tubes on the table.

Rio quickly looked around the room before lightly frowning.

“Can I open the window?” she asked. “Because of the smell of paint.”

Agatha immediately let out an amused little sigh.

“Rio, I’m fine.”

“Even so, the smell of paint could be harmful.” Carmen smiled faintly while wiping her hands on a cloth.

“You can open it, darling.”

Rio walked toward the large cabin windows and partially opened them, allowing the cool forest breeze inside along with the distant sound of birds.

Agatha watched her for a few seconds before shaking her head, amused.

“She became completely overprotective.” Carmen immediately laughed softly.

“That’s to be expected,” she replied naturally. “Rio loves in a very… intense way. She always has.”

“Mom, don’t ruin my reputation,” Rio complained immediately while returning to the sofa.

Agatha laughed quietly.

Carmen let out an entertained laugh while watching her daughter’s offended expression.

“Darling… you ruined your own reputation long before that.”

“See?” Agatha murmured playfully. “Finally someone sane in this family.”

Rio narrowed her eyes at the two of them.

She sat beside Agatha shortly after, sliding one hand to the side of her belly in such a natural gesture that it felt involuntary at this point.

Carmen watched that in silence for a moment, with affection and maybe even a certain surprise.

Because that definitely was not the Rio she had known for most of her life.

“How are you, Mom?” Rio asked then, her voice softer.

Carmen sighed slowly before answering honestly:

“There are difficult days,” she admitted. “Some very difficult ones, actually.”

She lowered her eyes to her hands for a moment before continuing:

“But I think this time will be different.”

Rio remained silent, listening, Carmen then smiled faintly.

“Now I have extra motivation.” Her eyes drifted directly to Agatha’s belly.

That immediately made the omega smile emotionally.

She then picked up the white-and-yellow envelope she had brought and held it out to Carmen.

“Actually… we came to bring you this.”

Carmen carefully took the invitation between her fingers.

She slowly opened the envelope and immediately smiled when she saw the small rabbits drawn on the invitation.

“Rabbits?” Agatha let out a soft little laugh.

“Rio nicknamed them that because they like jumping around inside my belly all the time.”

Rio gently caressed Agatha’s belly right after, as if the babies themselves had been called into the conversation.

“They’re our little bunnies,” she murmured quietly, almost distractedly.

Then she looked up at her mother.

“And we’d be very happy if you came.”

Carmen spent a few seconds staring at the invitation in silence, her eyes discreetly filling with tears before she nodded.

“I’ll be there.” Her voice came out emotional.

“Thank you for inviting me.” Agatha immediately held her hand gently.

“We want you to be part of your grandchildren’s lives.” Carmen blinked quickly trying to hold back tears.

Agatha squeezed her hand once more before softly adding:

“You deserve that.” That seemed to hit Carmen for real.

She turned her face away for a moment, taking a deep breath before looking back at them.

“Thank you so much, girls…” she said emotionally. “Really.”

Rio took a deep breath after a few seconds of silence.

Her fingers still absentmindedly caressed the side of Agatha’s belly while Carmen watched the two of them from the small table crowded with brushes, canvases, and scattered paint tubes.

The wind coming through the open windows made the pale cabin curtains sway softly, carrying away part of the strong paint smell.

“Mom… there’s something else I need to talk to you about,” Rio finally said.

Carmen immediately looked up at her.

“Alright… what happened?”

Rio exchanged a quick glance with Agatha before continuing.

“I hired a detective a few weeks ago,” she began slowly. “I thought it was important to investigate some things about the family… especially now because of the babies. I want to make sure they’re legally protected in the future.”

Carmen remained silent, listening carefully.

Rio took another deep breath.

“Some of Abuela Claire’s documents weren’t making sense to me… especially her will. So I started investigating it more deeply.”

Carmen’s expression subtly changed.

“What kind of thing?”

Rio rested her forearms on her legs, clearly thinking about how to phrase it.

“The detective found two versions of the will,” she explained. “There’s an amendment registered two days after Abuela Claire’s official date of death.”

Carmen blinked immediately, confused.

“What?”

Agatha looked at Rio, noticing her hesitation, and then calmly took over the conversation.

“Originally… you were supposed to inherit part of the company’s administration,” she explained carefully.

“But after that amendment, everything passed directly to the family alpha… which at the time was Rita.”

Carmen went completely still for a few seconds, her face slowly losing color.

“So my own mother Claire altered something… days after her own death… just to affect me?”

Rio lifted her eyes to look at her.

“That’s the thing, mom… if she was already dead, someone forged it. And that someone…” Carmen closed her eyes for a moment, as if she had known the answer for years.

“Mommy…” she murmured quietly. “I can imagine.”

She slowly stood from the chair and walked toward the chalet window. Outside, a few people strolled through the clinic’s tree-lined gardens while the wind moved through the tall Long Island trees.

Carmen stayed there for a few seconds simply staring at the landscape.

“How does she manage to become more horrible every single time…?”

Rio stood up too.

“The detective is still investigating everything from that time. But if there really was fraud… we can sue her.”

Carmen let out a small bitter laugh.

“As far as I’m concerned, do whatever you need to do. You have my blessing.”

She crossed her arms around herself before continuing:

“I tried, Rio… I tried to escape that family. I ran away with your father… tried to build another life.”

Agatha watched Rio lower her eyes discreetly while Carmen took a deep breath.

“But life brought me back to that house.”

Her voice slowly started to crack.

“I had you… and I was pregnant with your sisters. I needed to guarantee some stability. After your father died, nobody wanted to hire me because she destroyed my entire reputation.”

Carmen’s eyes filled with tears.

“Your grandmother told the whole city I was unstable. That I was ungrateful. That I had killed Claire. And anyone who dared to hire me… she threatened to destroy.”

Agatha felt her chest tighten painfully.

“So I had to go back.” Carmen continued softly. “Go back to living with her… and subject you girls to all of that.”

Rio crossed the small space between them immediately.

“Mom…” she said quietly, carefully holding her mother’s shoulders.

“You did the best you could in your situation.” Carmen lowered her eyes.

Rio continued:

“Despite everything… you gave us a roof over our heads. You gave me and my sisters stability. You protected us the best way you could.”

Carmen breathed deeply, trying to hold back the tears, then Rio took her hands.

“And I’m going to find out the whole truth.” she said in a low voice. “And if she did something wrong… she’s going to be held accountable.”

For a few seconds Carmen simply stared at her daughter, as if she still struggled to believe that the quiet, wounded little girl had grown enough to stand up against Rita Vidal.

Then she slowly nodded before walking toward the dresser positioned near the chalet wall.

She opened the bottom drawer and pulled out a small old dark wooden box. Agatha immediately noticed the change in Rio’s face.

Carmen held the box in her hands for a few more seconds before fully handing it to Rio.

Her fingers trembled lightly.

“It belonged to my mother… Claire.” she said softly. “I never touched it. I never wanted to know what was inside.”

Rio accepted the box carefully, as if she was afraid it might break.

“But maybe it’ll be useful to you now.” Carmen concluded, taking a deep breath.

Agatha nodded immediately.

“It’ll help a lot.”

The chalet fell silent for a few seconds.

Rio ran her thumb across the old lid of the box, observing the tiny details worn down by time.

There was something deeply strange about that moment. For years Claire Vidal had seemed to exist only as a ghost inside that family, like a whispered name, an old portrait.

A forbidden subject.

And now she was there, in Rio’s hands. Carmen slowly sat down again on the small couch near the window.

“Your grandmother hid everything.” she commented quietly. “Documents, letters, photographs. She used to say the past only existed to weaken people.”

Rio let out a humorless laugh.

“Of course she did.”

Agatha watched the alpha silently, noticing how her jaw was tense again, but different from the anger she usually carried whenever she talked about her family.

This felt like something else.

Grief.

Maybe for the first time Rio was realizing Claire wasn’t just some distant piece in the history of the Vidal family.

She had been someone. A real woman. A mother. And maybe a victim too.

Agatha slowly stepped closer, taking Rio’s free hand.

The alpha immediately intertwined their fingers instinctively, the way she always did.

“We’re not going through this alone.” Agatha said softly.

Rio lifted her eyes toward her, and something in her expression softened instantly.

Carmen watched them silently, her fingers subtly tightening around her own dress while she saw her daughter being touched with a tenderness she perhaps had never received before in her life.

“She loves you very much.” Carmen suddenly murmured to Agatha.

Rio almost looked embarrassed.

“Mom…”

“I’m serious.” Carmen insisted with a small tired smile. “Rio never let anyone get this close to her before.”

Agatha looked at Rio for a moment, then smiled softly.

“I know.”

Rio discreetly turned her face away, but Agatha felt her fingers tighten around her hand a little harder.

◆

A few hours later, the three of them were at the clinic’s main restaurant.

The place looked more like a luxury hotel than a rehabilitation center. Huge glass windows displayed the forests surrounding Long Island while the soft late afternoon light reflected against the golden details of the elegant dining hall.

Agatha finished her juice and placed her hands on the table to slowly stand up.

“I’m going to get more food before these two start a revolution in here.” she murmured while rubbing her belly.

Rio immediately lifted her eyes from her plate.

“Take it easy on the carbs, sweetheart.”

Agatha stopped mid-step and slowly turned to look at her.

“Rio Vidal…” she said incredulously. “I’m eating for three.”

Rio ended up smiling. Carmen did too.

“I think I lost the right to worry about nutritional balance the moment I became a human incubator.”

Agatha finished before walking toward the buffet. Rio watched her walk away for a few seconds.

She watched the soft yellow dress and her rounded belly.

The instinctive way one of Agatha’s hands remained resting beneath her stomach while she walked.

It was impossible not to stare, and  Carmen noticed immediately, she let out a small tired, but happy sigh.

“Thank you for today… seriously.” she said quietly. “It was wonderful.”

Rio looked away from Agatha and toward her mother, her expression softening immediately.

“It was really good seeing you too, mom.”

Carmen nodded, absentmindedly playing with the napkin.

“My sisters, have you seen them?”

“Ester and Lizzie were here last week… but you know how it is.” she commented. “They’re completely focused on the weddings.”

Rio let out a small nasal laugh.

“I can imagine the chaos.”

“Whether we like it or not…” Carmen continued while looking out the window, “the best thing we ever did for them was keep them away from all of this.”

Rio stayed quiet for a moment before nodding.

“Yeah.”

Then Carmen looked directly at her.

“And you?” she asked casually. “Do you think about getting married?”

Rio nearly choked on her own water.

“Mom…”

“What?” Carmen shrugged playfully. “You’re obviously hopelessly in love with her.”

Rio ran a hand over her face, letting out a long sigh, but she didn’t deny it. Not even for a second.

“If I could…” she started quietly, “I’d marry her right here at this buffet.”

A small smile instantly appeared on Carmen’s face. Rio lowered her eyes to her hands before continuing.

“But I need to wait for the pregnancy… all of this. I don’t want to pressure her. Not now… not while she’s still figuring out how broken I am.”

Carmen’s expression softened immediately.

“You’re not broken, my daughter. You’ve just been through a lot.”

Rio let out a humorless little laugh.

“So has she, mom. Agatha’s family isn’t easy either… she left home very young. I don’t want to force anything on her.”

Carmen nodded slowly.

“I understand… but relax.” she said gently. “She doesn’t seem like someone who’s going to run away.”

“I know.” Rio smiled faintly.

Agatha came back a few minutes later balancing two small plates and a clearly improvised dessert stacked on top of them.

The yellow dress emphasized her already heavy belly even more as she carefully walked between the tables of the clinic’s elegant restaurant.

Rio watched the entire scene attentively.

“I sincerely hope there’s some protein in the middle of all those carbs.” she commented as Agatha approached.

Agatha immediately rolled her eyes.

“Like I said before, my love, I’m eating for three.” she shot back while slowly sitting down in the chair. “And honestly, I think I lost the right to care about aesthetics a few months ago.”

“You look absolutely stunning, darling.”

“And besides,” Agatha continued while grabbing the fork, “your children apparently have the appetite of an entire wolf pack.”

“My children?” Rio raised an amused eyebrow. “So when they kick your bladder at three in the morning and I have to calm them down, they’re our children, but when they attack an entire buffet they suddenly become mine?”

“Exactly. Whenever they do something wrong, it’s your fault.”

Carmen ended up laughing quietly for the first time in a long while.

“I’m adopting that argument from now on.” she commented, amused. “It seems extremely useful.”

◆

As they left the clinic, the sky was already beginning to turn orange over Long Island. The cool air swept through the parking lot while Rio guided Agatha toward the car with a firm hand resting against her back.

As soon as they got in, Rio started the car and began driving back toward Manhattan. For a few minutes, the silence between them was comfortable.

Agatha absentmindedly watched the scenery passing by outside the window while rubbing her belly in slow circles. The babies were especially active after lunch, which caused little movements to appear beneath the fabric of the yellow dress.

Rio glanced quickly at her.

“You okay?”

“Mhm…” Agatha murmured with a small smile. “They just decided to throw a rave after the buffet.”

Rio let out a soft laugh. Then Agatha unlocked her phone.

“I need to buy a dress for the baby shower.” she commented while scrolling through online stores. “I was looking at some options…”

Rio immediately wrinkled her nose in the most dramatic way possible.

“Oh no. Forget those stores.”

Agatha slowly turned toward her.

“Excuse me?”

“We’re going to the mall.”

Agatha let out an incredulous laugh.

“What exactly is your prejudice against Amazon?”

“I hate waiting for things to be delivered.” Rio answered naturally while changing lanes. “If I want something, I just go to a store and buy it.”

Agatha’s eyes widened.

“That is absurdly more expensive.”

Rio shrugged without seeming to care in the slightest.

“I’ve never noticed.”

Agatha immediately smacked her lightly on the arm.

“My God, you’re insufferable.”

Rio only smiled, entirely pleased with herself.

And twenty minutes later, she was driving into the underground parking garage of a luxury mall in Manhattan.

As soon as she parked, she got out first and immediately walked around to open Agatha’s door.

“Madam.” she said theatrically. Agatha rolled her eyes while getting out of the car holding her purse.

“You’re ridiculous.”

“And yet you’re still with me.”

Rio closed the door behind her before placing a hand against the omega’s back, guiding her into the brightly lit mall.

The place was crowded even at that hour. Golden lights reflected off the pale marble floors while gigantic storefronts displayed absurdly expensive clothes Agatha didn’t even want to imagine the price of.

“Since we’re here…” Agatha started while unlocking her phone again, “we need to buy a few more things for the babies.”

Rio nodded immediately. Agatha opened a massive list of items written in her notes app.

Diapers.
 Baby bottles.
 Blankets.
 Bath products.
 Tiny clothes.

Rio looked over the list from above her shoulder.

“All of that?”

“There are two babies, Rio.”

“Right… fair.”

The two of them ended up walking into the first baby store in the hallway, and Rio simply stopped in the middle of the entrance, completely frozen. Agatha turned her head toward her, confused.

“What?”

Rio stared silently at the inside of the store.

The tiny furniture, strollers, the impossibly small clothes hanging everywhere and the ridiculously tiny shoes. Then she slowly took a deep breath, slightly overwhelmed.

“They’re going to be so small…” she murmured, more to herself than to Agatha.

The omega’s heart tightened immediately. Rio Vidal looked genuinely terrified of becoming a mother.

“They’re going to be small…” Rio repeated, staring at the tiny outfits hanging nearby.

Agatha smiled, slowly walking closer before picking up one of the miniature pieces in her hands.

“They are.” she said softly. “They have to fit in here first.”

She guided Rio’s hand to her belly, and the babies immediately moved beneath the alpha’s touch. Rio let out a breath through her nose, emotional.

“But they’ll grow.” Agatha continued. “And they’ll be beautiful… hopefully with your eyes.”

Rio slowly turned her face toward her, her entire expression softening.

“I hope they take after you.”

That made Agatha smile. Then she unlocked her phone and handed it to Rio.

“Hold the list.”

Rio obeyed without complaining, resting her arms on the shopping cart while starting to read the items written down.

“Alright… clothes first.”

Agatha immediately started walking through the aisles, carefully analyzing the fabrics.

“I want neutral tones.” she explained while pulling a few hangers forward. “White, yellow, beige… things like that.”

Rio nodded.

“Alright. But I liked these.”

She pointed at a set covered in tiny giraffes, and then another embroidered with classic little teddy bears.

Agatha ended up laughing.

“Of course you picked the most expensive ones in the store.”

“They have tiny teddy bears wearing ties.”

“Rio…”

“That is objectively adorable.”

Agatha still grabbed two of each and tossed them into the cart anyway.

As they kept moving down the list, Rio continued reading the items out loud.

“Thermal blankets… bottles… diapers… ointments…”

Then she paused for a second and read naturally:

“Breast pump.”

Agatha froze in the middle of the aisle.

Rio immediately lifted her eyes from the phone, noticing the blush spreading across her face.

“What?”

“Rio…” Agatha murmured, embarrassed. “That’s not even necessary yet.”

Rio raised an eyebrow, clearly amused.

“Of course it is.”

Agatha immediately looked away, pretending to inspect another shelf, which only made Rio find the situation even funnier.

“Are you embarrassed?”

Agatha huffed quietly.

“A little.”

“About breastfeeding?”

“Rio…”

This time the alpha smiled with genuine tenderness.

“Why?” she asked, stepping closer to her. “It’s something natural, isn’t it?”

Agatha sighed in defeat.

“Because I feel like I’m going to turn into a dairy cow.”

Rio tried to hold back her laughter and failed miserably.

“You’re going to be an extremely sexy dairy cow, I’ll admit that.”

“Idiot.”

Agatha lightly smacked her again while Rio finally walked closer, carefully holding the omega by the shoulders.

“My love…” she said more quietly this time, “you don’t need to worry about that. It’s a completely natural process. And you have absolutely no reason to feel ashamed.”

Agatha lowered her eyes a little.

“I know it’s silly…”

Rio immediately shook her head.

“Your feelings are not silly.” she answered firmly. “And you can talk to me whenever you feel like this, okay?”

Agatha nodded slowly.

Rio then looked back toward the shelf and, without hesitation, grabbed a Medela box that was almost five hundred dollars more expensive than the regular brand breast pump.

Agatha’s eyes widened immediately.

“Rio! That costs almost a kidney!”

“Perfect.” she placed it in the cart without even blinking. “I still have two working.”

Agatha laughed, rolling her eyes while the two of them walked toward the checkout counter of the baby store.

Before she even managed to pull her wallet out of her purse, Rio had already handed her black card to the cashier.

Agatha let out a defeated sigh.

“Rio… I could pay for some things.”

The alpha merely raised one eyebrow slowly.

Agatha immediately lifted her hands.

“Okay, okay… I know.”

Rio smiled, satisfied, while signing the receipt.

Agatha then stepped closer to her and quickly kissed the tip of the alpha’s nose. The gesture was so spontaneous that Rio actually blinked in surprise for a second.

“Get used to this, sweetheart.” Rio murmured while putting the card back into her wallet. “I’m always going to pay for everything.”

Agatha shrugged theatrically.

“If I get spoiled, it’s your fault.”

Rio held all the bags in one hand before looking at her in that dangerously intense way.

“That’s my greatest desire.”

Agatha ended up laughing again, and then the two of them left the store walking side by side through the crowded mall.

“Now I need to find a dress for the baby shower.” Agatha commented while looking at some store windows.

Rio gave her a slow glance, one that lingered far too long.

“It’s going to be wonderful seeing your choice.”

The tone carried such an openly suggestive edge that Agatha immediately narrowed her eyes.

“Rio.”

“What?” she asked, far too innocently. “I’m talking about the dress.”

“You definitely are not.”

Rio only smiled.

A few minutes later, Agatha walked into an elegant maternity store. The atmosphere was bright and sophisticated, filled with light fabrics and soft colors spread across the racks.

As soon as they walked in, a smiling saleswoman approached them.

“Good afternoon, may I help you?”

“Yes.” Agatha answered politely. “I’m looking for a dress for my baby shower.”

The woman immediately looked excited.

“Oh, of course! We’ve had several beautiful new options arrive this week, come with me.”

Agatha nodded and followed the saleswoman through the aisles of the store.

Rio watched the two of them walk away before she started wandering distractedly through the rest of the space.

Her eyes drifting over the pieces.

Dresses, robes, and comfortable postpartum clothes, then she stopped, looking exclusively at one piece, a long silk nightgown had caught her attention.

Thin and sleeveless, with a delicate fit.

The fabric flowed elegantly all the way down to the floor in a soft cream shade, Rio stared at the piece for a few seconds, desperately trying to maintain thoughts that were even remotely respectable.

And failed miserably, because she immediately imagined Agatha wearing it, her rounded belly, loose hair and the silk sliding over her skin, clinging to her curves as she walked through the apartment.

The alpha closed her eyes for a moment, breathing deeply while fighting against getting an erection in the middle of a maternity clothing store.

“My God…” she murmured to herself, she definitely needed to see Agatha in that nightgown, desperately needed to.

Rio then grabbed the piece from the rack almost without thinking, and at that exact moment she heard Agatha’s voice coming from the fitting room:

“Rio? The zipper got stuck!”

Rio spent a few seconds just staring at the fitting room curtain, as if she had completely forgotten where she was.

The small fitting room, the half-open curtain almost inviting, Rio stepped inside and nothing else seemed to truly exist besides Agatha standing in front of her.

The white dress hugged the omega’s body delicately, softly outlining the rounded curve of her pregnancy without hiding any of it. The pale fabric fell lightly over her thighs, with that elegant vintage cut that made Agatha look like she had stepped out of another era.

Agatha looked far too beautiful.

And dangerously fucking hot.

Rio slowly moved closer behind her, eyes sliding over the bare skin of her back partially exposed by the dress. Tiny freckles scattered across her pale skin made the alpha hold her breath for a second.

Then she tilted her face down, brushing her nose slowly against Agatha’s neck while her fingers reached the open zipper.

Agatha shivered immediately.

Rio felt it beneath her hands.

The zipper slid up slowly, inch by inch, while she stayed far too close, breathing her scent in like she needed it to keep functioning.

“What do you think?” Agatha asked softly.

Rio lifted her eyes to her reflection in the mirror, and something inside Rio ached, because Agatha looked exactly like the kind of dream someone would invent knowing they could never actually have it.

But Rio did.

She had Agatha in her hands, had their children coming soon, and sometimes it still felt impossible.

The alpha slid one hand to Agatha’s waist, spreading her fingers slowly over the white fabric while the other rose to her neck.

“You’re perfect…” she answered hoarsely.

Agatha smiled faintly at their reflection, small and shy.

But Rio immediately noticed the way she blushed.

“Perfect even this pregnant?” she teased quietly.

Rio let out a soft laugh through her nose before lowering her face to the curve of Agatha’s shoulder and leaving a slow, lingering kiss there.

“Especially like this,” she murmured against her skin. “You have no idea what you’re doing to me wearing this dress.”

“Rio…”

“I’m serious.” the alpha answered immediately, fingers tightening lightly on her waist. “Because if you show up to the baby shower looking like this, I’m going to spend hours pretending to have social etiquette while thinking about ripping this dress off the second we get home.”

Agatha let out a muffled laugh, shaking her head.

“You’re impossible.”

“And completely obsessed with you,” Rio replied without hesitation.

That made Agatha’s heart stumble inside her chest, because Rio said things like that with terrifying honesty.

As if loving Agatha that way was simply an unavoidable fact of the universe.

The omega then partially turned within her arms, ending up face to face with Rio in the cramped fitting room space. Her hands slowly slid over the alpha’s dark blazer.

“Then I guess I’m taking this one,” she whispered, amused.

Rio smiled immediately.

“Excellent choice, sweetheart.”

Then she tilted her face down again, kissing Agatha slowly right there in the middle of the fitting room, as if the rest of the world could wait a few more minutes.

Agatha held her breath when she heard that.

“Excellent choice.”

“I’ll help you take it off…” she whispered against Rio’s mouth.

The alpha immediately lost what little self-control she still had left.

Agatha slowly turned her back to her again, and Rio caught the zipper between her fingers, pulling it down slowly along her spine while watching the white fabric gradually open.

The dress slid smoothly down the omega’s body until it fell at her feet, and Rio stared for a second.

Her omega standing there in nothing but lingerie, with her rounded belly and milky skin marked by thin stretch marks, tiny freckles scattered across her shoulders.

Agatha was beautiful in an almost offensive way.

So Rio simply couldn’t take it anymore.

She surged forward against Agatha, gripping her waist firmly while attacking her neck with deep, lingering kisses, breathing hard against her skin like someone completely addicted to it.

Agatha gasped immediately, hands clutching Rio’s dark blazer.

“Rio… the saleswomen…”

The alpha slowly lifted her eyes, still pressed against her, completely affected by her own pheromones.

“Doesn’t matter…” she murmured hoarsely against her mouth. “I own shares in this fucking mall. Nobody would dare come in here.”

Rio let out a small nervous laugh before sighing when Rio went back to kissing her throat.

Her entire body seemed to melt inside the alpha’s arms, and Rio smiled against her skin.

“I saw a nightgown…” she murmured softly. “Beautiful.”

Agatha blinked, still a little dazed from her scent.

“A nightgown…?”

“Yes.” Rio replied, slowly sliding her hands along her waist. “And I really want to see you wearing it.”

The tone of the alpha’s voice immediately sent heat rushing up Agatha’s face. Rio rested her forehead against hers for a moment, her dark eyes completely locked onto her.

“You have no idea how insane you make me like this… pregnant with my pups… wearing pretty things for me.”

Agatha bit her lip, trying to breathe normally while Rio scattered a few more slow kisses along her neck and shoulder.

“You’re completely obsessed…” she whispered through a shaky smile.

Rio smiled back without a hint of shame.

“Yes. Completely.”

Rio slowly slid her hand along the inside of Agatha’s thigh, feeling her whole body shiver before the touch even fully happened.

Then her fingers found the heat between the omega’s legs, and Agatha let out a trembling sigh immediately, her forehead falling against Rio’s shoulder as she pressed herself even closer to her.

Her sensitive body reacting entirely to the alpha’s touch alone.

“Rio…” it came out soft, almost needy.

Rio closed her eyes for a second, completely intoxicated by her reaction.

By her scent, by the way Agatha simply melted in her arms.

Her fingers slowly stroked over the fabric of her panties, feeling the omega’s pulsing core react instantly to the contact.

Agatha arched slightly, trapping a muffled moan against Rio’s neck.

“So sensitive…” Rio murmured hoarsely near her ear. “Is all of this because of me?”

Agatha let out a breathless little laugh, completely flushed.

“You know it is…”

Rio smiled dangerously when she heard that, then tightened her grip on Agatha’s waist with her other hand, keeping her firmly pressed against her body while she scattered more slow kisses along her jawline.

“I really should buy that nightgown…” she murmured softly, still touching her slowly. “Because honestly? I think I’d lose my mind watching you walk around the apartment pregnant and sleeping in silk.”

Agatha hid her face in Rio’s neck with a muffled laugh.

“You really are hopeless.”

“No.” Rio answered immediately. “And it’s entirely your fault.”

Rio lost the rest of her composure the moment she felt Agatha unconsciously grind against her hand.

The alpha slowly slipped her fingers inside her panties, immediately feeling the omega’s wet heat pulse against her touch. It didn’t take long before she slid her fingers inside Agatha’s pussy, making her gasp.

Her entire body tensing before melting against Rio all over again.

“Fuck…” she whispered, eyes falling shut.

Rio let out a heavy breath against her neck, completely affected by the reaction. Her fingers began stroking her slowly, teasing, sliding in and out while her thumb rubbed circles over Agatha’s clit.

Agatha trembled all over in her arms, gripping the dark blazer tightly while trying to hold back her sounds.

“Rio… someone could hear…”

“Then stay quiet for me…” the alpha murmured hoarsely against her mouth.

That made Agatha shiver completely. Rio kept the movements slow and firm, fascinated as she watched every reaction reflected in the fitting room mirror.

The rounded belly, her flushed face, her heavy pleasure-filled eyes.

Agatha was beautiful. Completely beautiful.
