“You have no idea what you do to me…” Rio confessed softly, her voice thick with desire. “I look at you like this and I feel like I’m going insane.”

Agatha whimpered quietly when Rio’s fingers pressed deeper into the omega’s tight pussy, making her immediately bury her face in the alpha’s shoulder.

“So obedient…” Rio whispered. “So pretty falling apart for me.”

Agatha gripped the alpha’s blazer tightly, hiding a muffled moan against her shoulder when Rio deepened the touch, teasing her slowly like she wanted to memorize every reaction from her body.

Her thumb kept stroking her clit in firm circular motions while Rio rested her forehead against hers, breathing heavily.

“So beautiful…” she murmured hoarsely. “You get so sensitive when I touch you like this…”

Agatha trembled again, completely melted against the alpha’s body.

“My love… please…”

That destroyed whatever was left of Rio’s self-control.

Rio kissed her again immediately, swallowing the soft sounds escaping Agatha’s mouth while holding her firmly by the waist, possessive as hell. The fitting room suddenly felt too small to contain the heat between them.

Rio gently held Agatha by the neck, fingers spread along the nape of her neck while keeping them pressed tightly together inside the tiny fitting room. She slowly turned Agatha around so her back was against her, making Agatha’s ass brush against Rio’s erection.

Rio closed her eyes for a second at the feeling.

“Fuck…” she murmured hoarsely against her neck.

Agatha braced her hands against the mirror, breathing harder and harder while Rio left slow kisses along her neck and shoulder.

The alpha kept one hand firm on her waist, guiding the movement of her fingers inside her while using Agatha’s movement against herself slowly, driven insane by the way she reacted.

Agatha ended up moaning louder when the pleasure finally hit her hard, her whole body giving in as her eyes fell shut. Immediately Rio lifted her hand to Agatha’s mouth, muffling the sound gently.

“Shhh…” she whispered near her ear, still breathless.

At that exact moment, both of them heard the saleswoman’s voice from outside the fitting room.

“Ma’am? Is everything alright in there?”

Agatha froze instantly, completely red.

Rio, on the other hand, had to bite back a smile, because that absolutely should not have been funny.

But seeing Agatha like that, trying to catch her breath while staring at her in horror, was way too adorable.

“Everything’s perfect.” Rio answered calmly without changing her tone of voice. “We just had a little problem with the zipper.”

Agatha turned to look at her in disbelief while Rio simply kissed the side of her head, still holding her by the waist.

Completely satisfied with herself.

◆

The day of the baby shower arrived beneath an absurdly blue sky over New York. After weeks of preparation, arguments over decorations, Jen’s outrageous choices, and Rio’s perfectionism, everything was finally ready.

Wanda and Jen had managed to convince Rio to rent the private garden behind an elegant museum in Manhattan, a space almost hidden from the rest of the city. The place was surrounded by tall trees, classical sculptures, and pale stone pathways covered in delicate petals.

The entire decoration looked like a tiny vintage paradise. Balloons in buttery white and soft yellow floated between floral arches, while classic little rabbits adorned the centerpieces.

Lace tablecloths covered long tables filled with delicate desserts, antique porcelain, and soft floral arrangements. Small golden lights hung between the trees even during the daytime, giving everything a magical atmosphere.

Agatha still had her eyes covered by Rio’s hands as they walked through the quiet interior of the museum.

“Rio, I’m going to trip and fall pregnant with twins onto the floor.” Agatha complained between laughs.

“I am literally holding you.” Rio answered calmly behind her.

“That means nothing coming from the woman who almost caused a car accident because she got distracted by a nightgown in a storefront.”

Rio let out an offended cough.

“That was completely out of context.”

Agatha laughed, and then they finally crossed the glass doors leading to the garden. Rio stopped behind her and slowly removed her hands from Agatha’s eyes.

Agatha blinked a few times, adjusting to the brightness before truly seeing everything.

She lost her breath the moment she took in the decorations. The garden looked illuminated in an unreal way, the soft colors blending with the green of the trees, making everything feel too delicate, too welcoming.

As if the entire place had been built specifically to welcome their babies.

Agatha’s eyes filled instantly.

“Oh my God…” she whispered, looking around. “This is beautiful…”

Rio smiled immediately at her reaction.

In the middle of the garden, Wanda and Jen practically came running toward them. Both of them wore matching yellow dresses, completely committed to their roles as emotional aunts.

“SURPRIIIISE!” Jen practically screamed, throwing her arms open.

Agatha immediately started laughing emotionally.

“You two are completely insane…”

“And incredible.” Wanda corrected, pointing at herself.

Agatha walked toward them, hugging Wanda first and then Jen, but ended up pulling both of them into the same embrace when she started crying again.

Hormones. So many hormones.

“Hey, hey, hey, don’t start or I’m gonna start too.” Jen complained, already sniffling.

“Too late.” Wanda answered, wiping at her own eyes.

Rio watched the scene from a few steps away with such a soft expression that almost nobody would believe that was Rio Vidal.

A few minutes later the guests began arriving little by little, filling the garden with voices, laughter, and the soft sound of the string quartet Wanda had insisted on hiring “because the babies deserved class.”

The first to arrive was Alice accompanied by Lorna. Alice wore an elegant wine-colored dress while Lorna carried two enormous gift boxes wrapped in pale yellow paper.

“I hate all of you for making me come to this on a Saturday before noon.” Alice complained the second she got close to them.

“You literally chose the time of the party.” Rio replied dryly.

“And I’m still complaining.”

Agatha laughed when Alice hugged her carefully because of the belly.

“You look gorgeous. Annoyingly gorgeous while pregnant, actually.”

“Thank you… I think.”

Lorna then handed the gifts to one of the absurdly organized staff members Rio had hired for the event, because of course Rio Vidal had an entire team dedicated solely to organizing baby shower presents.

Jen said that was “psychotic on astronomical levels.”

Soon after, some of Agatha’s college friends arrived, along with people close to Wanda and Jen, plus Loki and Sylvie, who immediately started arguing over which baby would have “the most potential for chaos.”

“If they take after Agatha, we’re fucked.” Loki said.

“That was offensive.” Agatha complained.

“And true.” Wanda replied while drinking non-alcoholic champagne.

The garden quickly filled up.

A few minutes later Carmen arrived accompanied by her younger daughters. Lizzie and Ester crossed the garden almost immediately toward Rio, hugging their older sister with genuine affection.

The three of them looked absurdly alike, the same intense eyes and elegant Vidal family aura, but unlike Rio, her sisters still carried a lightness she had never fully possessed.

“So this is the famous Agatha?” Lizzie asked immediately when she looked at her.

Agatha laughed awkwardly.

“I guess so?”

Ester practically lit up when she saw her.

“Oh my God, you’re even prettier in person.”

Rio closed her eyes slowly.

“Can you two behave normally for five minutes?”

“No.” they answered in unison.

Agatha started laughing.

The sisters immediately began asking a dozen questions at once about the babies, the pregnancy, the names, and how Rio was behaving at home.

“Does she really make you sit down every twenty minutes?” Lizzie asked in horror.

“Yes.” Agatha answered seriously.

“She literally banned me from climbing chairs.”

“Because you tried climbing onto a chair seven months pregnant to hang a painting on the wall.” Rio shot back.

“Details.”

Carmen watched everything from a few steps away, smiling quietly. Maybe because it had been a very long time since she had seen Rio like this.

She approached them, kissing her daughter’s cheek gently before lovingly touching Agatha’s belly.

“You two created something beautiful together…” she murmured emotionally.

Rio simply held her mother’s hand for a moment.

Agatha had started talking to some guests near the main table while holding a glass of lemonade when something on the other side of the garden caught her attention.

And the world seemed to slow down.

Her brother was walking beside Aunt Myrtle, and between them was Evanora.

Agatha froze completely.

The air seemed to vanish from her lungs as she watched her mother cross the elegant garden filled with rabbits, flowers, and soft colors like a shadow invading something far too delicate.

Instinctively, Rio felt the change, because a second later she was already beside Agatha, her hand immediately resting against her back.

“My love… are you okay?”

The omega took a few seconds to answer.

Jackson approached first, hugging his sister carefully because of the belly before whispering near her ear:

“I didn’t think she’d actually come.”

Agatha only nodded slowly, still tense.

Myrtle gave her a gentler hug, emotional at seeing her pregnant, but Evanora remained standing a few steps away simply watching everything.

Just like she always did.

Agatha took a deep breath before finally looking directly at her.

“Mom… I didn’t think you were coming.” she said calmly.

Evanora lifted her chin slightly.

“I’m not a monster.” she replied. “Even though I completely disapprove of your lifestyle… I’m here.”

Rio immediately stiffened beside her.

Agatha felt it. It was like an electric current moving through the alpha’s body.

Then Rio stepped forward.

Dangerously calm.

“Rio Vidal. Pleasure to meet you.” she said, extending her hand.

Evanora looked surprised as she shook it. Her eyes traveled over Rio from head to toe before narrowing slightly.

“You are…?”

Rio did not hesitate for a second.

“Agatha’s girlfriend.” she answered firmly. “And the mother of her children.”

The silence afterward was almost comical.

Evanora literally froze for a few seconds before slowly narrowing her eyes.

“I thought you were that woman from the mall…”

Agatha closed her eyes briefly.

“Alice is a friend.” Agatha answered, her voice dangerously controlled. “And she helped me avoid being publicly humiliated by you that day.”

Evanora looked offended.

“I was trying to protect your reputation, Agatha.”

“No.” Rio cut in immediately. “You were trying to humiliate your daughter. I know the entire story.”

That made Myrtle hold her breath.

Jackson literally looked up at the sky like he was begging for patience.

Evanora turned her eyes back to Rio.

“Do you even know who she is? Do you truly know my daughter?”

The question dripped with judgment.

As if Agatha were too complicated. Too difficult.

Rio let out a humorless nasal laugh.

Then she answered:

“I do. And honestly? I think it’s impressive that someone made her believe she needed to be less than what she is.”

Agatha immediately felt her eyes burn.

Because Rio never looked away for even a second while saying it.

Evanora clearly did not like that.

“So, ma’am, I’m asking you for a little more respect toward my girlfriend and the mother of my pups.”

Rio continued, her tone now far too calm not to sound threatening.

“You are still welcome here as long as you treat Agatha properly.”

Then Rio took a subtle step closer.

“But if you cross the line, you’ll leave on the same bus you arrived on.”

Evanora fell silent.

Even Wanda, on the other side of the garden, slowly lowered her glass when she noticed the tension.

Evanora stayed quiet for several seconds after Rio’s veiled threat.

Then something unexpected happened.

She took a deep breath and relaxed her rigid posture slightly.

“Alright…” she finally said. “I apologize.”

Agatha blinked in surprise.

Even Myrtle looked shocked.

“I didn’t come here to ruin anything.” Evanora continued more quietly. “Despite my opinions… I know today is important to you.”

Jackson immediately grabbed his mother’s arm discreetly, almost as if he were ready to drag her away if she made things worse again.

“Let’s go get something to drink.” he said quickly, already pulling Evanora away.

Myrtle gave Agatha a small apologetic look before following them.

As soon as they walked away, Agatha finally released the breath trapped in her lungs.

Her hands were trembling slightly, and Rio noticed immediately.

The alpha carefully held her shoulders, bringing their faces closer together.

“Hey… take a deep breath for me.” she murmured softly. “It’s okay.”

Agatha nodded slowly, trying to obey, but Rio could hear her heartbeat racing even through the sound of conversations and the soft music filling the garden.

So fast and nervous.

Rio leaned in and kissed her forehead slowly before brushing away the fresh tear that had slipped down her face with her thumb, and the gesture nearly made Agatha fall apart all over again.

Because no one had ever stood between her and Evanora like that before.

Wanda appeared a few seconds later holding a glass of water.

“Here.” she said, handing it to Agatha. “Before you pass out and ruin the makeup Jen spent an hour doing.”

Agatha let out a weak laugh through the nerves and took the glass.

Rio glanced at Wanda briefly.

“Thank you.”

Wanda shrugged.

“If your grandmother and her mother ever decide to show up at the same event again, I’m charging extra for psychological damage.”

“Don’t worry, my grandmother is enjoying her European summer with some escort on a yacht. She won’t be showing up anytime soon.”

“Fascinating lifestyle choice,” Jen commented, very obviously mocking her.

That pulled a genuine laugh out of Agatha this time, and Rio sighed in relief when she finally felt Agatha’s body relax a little against hers.

Then her hand slid down to Agatha’s rounded belly, and immediately she felt a hard kick beneath her palm. The alpha arched a brow.

“I think the rabbits got stressed too.”

Agatha took a deep breath, rubbing her belly together with her.

“Sorry babies… mommy’s family is complicated.”

“Both families,” Wanda corrected.

Rio unfortunately nodded in agreement.

The alpha let out a small sigh when she felt another strong movement beneath her hand.

Then, with absolutely no shame about being surrounded by guests, she simply crouched down in front of Agatha.

Wanda immediately crossed her arms.

“Oh no… here we go.”

Agatha turned red instantly.

“Rio…”

But the alpha was already partially kneeling in front of her, completely focused on the rounded belly beneath the pale dress.

As if the rest of the garden had stopped existing.

Rio gently moved one of Agatha’s hands away from her stomach just so she could hold the belly with both hands first.

Then she lowered her head and kissed her belly over the fabric, slow and affectionate.

Another kick answered immediately.

Rio smiled softly, genuinely.

“Hey… easy, you two,” she murmured quietly to the babies. “Don’t scare mommy.”

Agatha felt her eyes burn with emotion all over again, because Rio did that naturally, without fear or embarrassment.

She simply did not care about anyone around them.

As if loving her and those babies was the most obvious thing in the world.

The alpha kissed her belly once more before resting the side of her face there for a few seconds.

Completely surrendered, treating Agatha like something far too precious to touch carelessly.

A genuine little treasure.

“You do realize you seem completely obsessed with me pregnant, right?” Agatha asked softly, trying to hold back her smile.

Rio lifted her eyes to her immediately, serious.

“Because I am.”

Wanda burst out laughing loudly.

“My God, she didn’t even try to deny it.”

“I would literally abandon billion-dollar meetings just to sit there watching her stomach move,” Rio replied, still kneeling.

“That’s terrifying,” Jen commented, appearing out of nowhere with a cupcake in hand.

Rio shrugged.

“And yet you all keep coming to my girlfriend’s apartment to eat the food I buy for her.”

“Because you’re rich and can buy more,” Jen shot back immediately.

Agatha laughed, and Rio slowly stood back up only to kiss the side of her belly one more time before pulling the omega against her waist again, still holding onto her like the entire world might try to take her away from her arms.

Chapter 15: Putting the Pieces Together

Chapter Notes

First, I want to thank all of you for the love and support. Ibiza is finally being talked about by word of mouth, and I’m so happy for every comment, every recommendation, and every person who takes a little time out of their day to follow this story. You are all very important to me 🩷

I also need to thank my pregnant friends, and mothers for this chapter. You were all incredibly sweet to me, especially my cousin, sharing your experiences with pregnancy exams and everything that comes with it. I wanted to explore that side a little more in this chapter, and mixing all of that with omegaverse ended up being really interesting to write.

Thank you Yas for telling me that glucose tolerance tests are horrible 😭 your experience definitely helped inspire this chapter, girl 🩷

So… I hope you all enjoy the chapter :)

 

┕──━──━─┑◆┍─━──━──┙

A few weeks had passed, and the baby shower pictures were still scattered around the apartment in little improvised picture frames Jen had insisted on leaving all over the place.

Every now and then, Agatha and Rio would end up laughing remembering the absurd games Wanda invented or Rio’s offended expression when she lost a round of “guess the baby food flavor.”

But what amused Agatha the most was something else entirely.

Rio had guessed practically every single gift correctly. Every one of them. Whether it was bottles or hygiene products, the car seats, even details Agatha hadn’t known existed.

“It’s terrifying how much you know about babies,” she commented days earlier while they organized some drawers in the nursery.

Rio didn’t even look up from the manual she was reading.

“I did research.”

“Rio… you know the difference between six different types of baby bottles.”

“Some of them have better airflow.”

Agatha just stared at her in silence.

“You are genuinely terrifying.” And now, that night, Rio was exactly how she’d been spending the last few weeks: Working.

Sitting on the massive bed in Agatha’s apartment, now practically overtaken by her belongings too.

Blazers occupied half the closet, medical books were scattered around the living room, and Rio’s perfume seemed to have seeped into every corner of the apartment.

The laptop rested open across her legs while she read another email from the private investigator.

The investigation was moving forward.

Too fast, actually, and that was exactly the problem.

The closer they got to the old Vidal family documents, the more doors started closing.

Legal blocks. “Lost” files. Employees who suddenly couldn’t talk anymore.

Even with Carmen immediately signing every authorization necessary for the investigation, there were still powerful hands protecting that family’s past, and Rio knew exactly whose hands those were.

But for now, her grandmother was in Europe, drifting between obscenely expensive hotels, private parties, and that gigantic fucking yacht where she disappeared for days at a time accompanied by women far too young and shamelessly interested in inheritance money.

When Rita did that, the entire world seemed to lose contact with her.

No phone turned on, zero replies, dangerously silent, Rio let out a tired sigh as she removed her glasses, rubbing her face slowly before leaning her head back.

She was exhausted, physically and mentally.

“Are you okay, my love?” Agatha’s voice came softly from in front of her.

Rio nodded.

“Yeah, I just—”

Then she stopped talking completely, because she finally looked up and  saw Agatha.

She was standing near the bedroom door wearing exactly the nightgown they’d bought in that store weeks earlier, the  fucking nightgown.

The thin silk slid over her body like clear water running down marble.

The pearly fabric flowed all the way to her feet in soft lines, tracing every new curve of her pregnancy without hiding any of them.

The open robe left her shoulders exposed, revealing pale skin marked by tiny freckles scattered across her collarbones and arms.

Her long hair fell loose down her back in soft dark waves, still slightly damp from the shower, and her eyes.

Fuck.

Those blue eyes of Agatha’s shone with that sleepy, warm look that always destroyed whatever rational thought Rio still had left.

The alpha simply forgot what she’d been saying, Forgot the email, forgot Rita, now she was somewhere in the back of her mind, so distant she felt like an uncertain memory.

Rio even forgot how to breathe properly for a few seconds.

“What?” Agatha asked, trying to suppress a smile.

“Is it really that bad?” she teased.

Rio let out a low, disbelieving laugh.

Then dragged a hand across her mouth while shamelessly looking Agatha up and down.

“My love…” her voice came out lower now. “You walked in looking like that and expected me to keep thinking about legal documents?”

Agatha bit the corner of her mouth, trying to hide her amusement, and that made everything worse.

“You did this on purpose,” she accused quietly.

“Maybe a little bit,” Agatha admitted.

Rio exhaled sharply through her nose.

“I’m going to die because of you someday.”

The next morning, Agatha was finishing getting ready in the bedroom while the golden early-morning light filtered through the apartment curtains.

The maternity workout set in shades of purple and white perfectly outlined her already heavy belly, and she tied her long hair into a high ponytail, watching in the mirror how her strands looked even stronger and shinier since the pregnancy.

The hormones had changed a lot about her, including that.

The classes with Natasha had started a few weeks earlier and, surprisingly, Agatha was taking everything extremely seriously ever since she decided she wanted a natural birth.

Rio supported it, and Lorna said the possibility was good and realistic, so the classes became her main focus.

Natasha also insisted heavily on preparing the alpha for the delivery, so Rio had been training alongside her, from massages to ease contractions to synchronized breathing techniques and the correct ways to support Agatha without causing tension.

Rio, of course, had practically turned it into a military mission, and Agatha still laughed remembering her taking notes in a small notebook during the first class.

She finished adjusting her sports top and walked into the living room, finding Rio already ready.

Sitting on the couch, dressed in dark workout clothes with her glasses resting on her face while attentively reading one of the journals found in the box Carmen had handed over weeks earlier, her fingers carefully held the old pages.

“All ready, baby?” Rio asked without taking her eyes off the journal.

“Yes.” Agatha approached slowly.

“So… making progress with the reading?”

Rio nodded slowly.

“I’m at the part where they met.”

Her voice came out quieter.

“Claire wrote about Rita as if she were… charming. Almost lovable. It’s strange. I can’t imagine my grandmother being the woman described here. Much less imagine her hurting her own wife considering the way Claire writes about her.”

Agatha sighed quietly, because she could. Or at least she could imagine someone slowly turning into something monstrous.

Rio closed the journal slowly before placing it back inside the box beside the couch.

“I’ll look at this later. Come on.”

Agatha nodded, Rio immediately picked up her bag before Agatha could even reach for it, naturally protective.

The two left the apartment in comfortable silence and headed to the parking garage and, as always, Rio opened the car door for Agatha first.

Only then did she walk around and get into the driver’s seat, but before starting the car, she picked up her phone.

“I need you to reschedule my five o’clock meeting for later,” she said in a short, professional voice message. “And send me the conference documents before lunch.”

Agatha stayed quiet in the passenger seat, watching.

She had no idea who Rio’s new assistant was, but unfortunately that alone was enough for an irritatingly possessive feeling to rise in the back of her chest. Small and stupid, but unfortunately very real.

Rio put her phone away and started the car without noticing the different kind of silence beside her.

“Natasha is probably going to make me do fifty squats today,” she commented distractedly while pulling out of the garage.

“Your assistant sounds efficient.” Agatha slowly crossed her arms.

Rio immediately turned her head toward her and then smiled dangerously from the corner of her mouth.

“You’re jealous, sweetheart?”

“Not even in your wildest dreams,” Agatha replied immediately, trying to sound indifferent.

Rio shot her an amused glance while driving.

“Mm. Of course.”

Agatha turned toward the window as if she genuinely didn’t care, and Rio nearly laughed.

“But yes, very efficient. Although I do have to scold them sometimes.”

Agatha shrugged again.

“I can imagine.”

The funniest part was that Rio’s “new assistant” was literally Billy Kaplan, a university beta majoring in business administration who already looked like he’d aged fifteen years working for Rio Vidal.

The poor guy lived surrounded by spreadsheets, impossible schedules, gigantic contracts, and meetings that started at six in the morning, and Rio honestly found it fascinating to watch his despair every time he received a new task.

She was actively enjoying torturing a poor college student during work hours.

And Agatha was clearly imagining something completely different.

Which made it even better.

Rio parked the car in the parking lot of the studio where Natasha taught the maternity classes and shut off the engine.

“Come on, jealous girl.”

“I am not jealous.”

“Of course you’re not.”

Rio got out before Agatha could respond and immediately walked around to open her door.

Agatha climbed out of the car with a quiet huff while adjusting the gym bag on her shoulder.

The two walked together toward the large building where the classes took place.

The place was cozy, filled with natural light from huge windows and decorated with soft tones and plants scattered through the corners.

Some pregnant women chatted near the reception desk while others were already sitting on giant pilates balls.

Agatha automatically noticed the different bellies, some small, others huge like hers.

One of the pregnant women was laughing while her husband tried to help her get off the floor, clearly failing.

Rio discreetly held Agatha’s hand while they walked, protective as always.

“Well, look who finally decided to show up,” Natasha commented, crossing her arms. “And not late today. I’m emotional.”

Agatha laughed.

“It was your controlling friend’s fault.” Rio raised an eyebrow.

“I prefer ‘organized.’”

Natasha let out a soft snort of laughter.

“Sure. Now go grab the mats because today we’re practicing positions to relieve lower back contractions and active breathing exercises.”

Rio immediately turned serious, like she was listening to instructions for surgery. Agatha had to hide her smile, because it was adorable seeing Rio Vidal treat childbirth classes like the most important event on the planet.

The two of them walked toward a quieter corner of the room, where several mats had already been arranged near the pilates balls and support cushions.

Rio immediately picked up a rolled mat she had brought with her.

Carefully separated from the others.

Agatha recognized the light purple mat instantly, still perfectly new, and smiled as she watched Rio kneel down to unroll it carefully on the floor.

“Rio…” she laughed softly. “We could’ve used the gym’s mats.”

“No.” Rio answered without hesitation.

She smoothed the edge of the mat so it sat perfectly straight before continuing:

“I bought this one to be exclusively yours.”

Agatha arched an amused eyebrow.

“Exclusively mine?”

“Yes. And purple because it’s your favorite color.”

Agatha ended up smiling wider at that before slowly sitting down on the mat.

“You’re impossible.”

Rio simply took off the light blazer she was wearing and settled behind her.

“Deal with it. Our cubs are never sharing toys. I fully intend to spoil both of them.”

Agatha laughed immediately.

“One day that spell is going to backfire on the witch, Vidal.”

Rio tilted her head.

“Impossible.”

“Oh yeah? Just wait until they figure out they can convince you to do anything by making sad faces.”

Rio looked genuinely thoughtful for a few seconds before sighing dramatically.

“Alright… maybe I’m in danger.”

Agatha burst into quiet laughter while Natasha started organizing the rest of the class.

Around them, some of the pregnant women were already stretching while their partners tried to follow the instructions without looking completely lost.

Natasha clapped her hands for attention.

“Today we’re practicing support positions during contractions and lower back pressure exercises. Partners, you’ll be working too, so no standing around just watching.”

Rio immediately focused again, posture straight and expression concentrated like she was about to participate in an international medical conference.

Agatha glanced sideways at her and had to hide another smile, because it was impossible not to love how absurdly serious Rio took every little thing about the pregnancy.

The class started a few minutes later, with Natasha walking around the room while calmly explaining the day’s exercises.

Rio handled everything with almost ridiculous seriousness.

Natasha first demonstrated lower back support techniques during contractions, teaching partners how to press specific points on the lower back to relieve pain.

Rio immediately sat behind Agatha on the purple mat, legs spread around her body while carefully placing her hands on the omega’s lower back.

“Here.” Natasha demonstrated on another pregnant woman. “Firm pressure, but steady.”

Rio copied it exactly.

Her large hands pressed slowly into Agatha’s lower back in circular motions while she remained seated between the alpha’s legs on the purple mat.

Agatha let out an immediate sigh.

“God… that feels amazing.”

Rio smiled subtly behind her, clearly satisfied that she was doing it right, while Agatha started practicing the breathing exercises Natasha had taught them.

Rio followed along too, almost synchronizing her breathing with hers.

Then came the stretches meant to help pelvic mobility and relieve pressure on the lower back.

Natasha demonstrated another position where the pregnant woman needed to lie down while the partner helped by moving her legs toward her body.

“This helps a lot with certain lower back pain and hip tension,” Natasha explained while demonstrating the movements.

Agatha lay down on the mat with a sigh while Rio positioned herself over her to help.

Her hands carefully held Agatha’s legs, body leaning over hers.

For one entire moment, their eyes met.

And immediately the flashbacks from the night before came rushing back, the slow kisses in the dark, Rio’s hands holding her thighs exactly like that.

It made her sigh.

The mood changed so fast Agatha actually held her breath.

Rio clearly did too.

Then a sound escaped Agatha.

Short.

Unmistakable.

A tiny fart right in the middle of the movement.

Agatha froze instantly.

Her eyes widened so much Rio had to bite the inside of her cheek to keep from laughing immediately.

For two entire seconds she tried to maintain composure, but then another sound escaped right after, a little louder this time.

Agatha covered her face with both hands in genuine despair.

“No… no, oh my God…”

Rio completely lost control.

The laugh burst out loud, making her shoulders shake while she lowered her head trying uselessly to stop laughing.

Agatha was already red up to her ears, absolutely horrified with herself while Natasha looked over her shoulder with the calmest expression in the world.

“Oh, perfect,” she commented professionally. “That position is excellent for releasing trapped gas during pregnancy.”

As if the universe had decided to humiliate Agatha a little more, a third one slipped out almost immediately afterward.

Natasha nodded in satisfaction like she was evaluating a perfectly executed physical exercise.

“Excellent. Agatha gets an A+ for today’s class.”

The entire room burst into laughter.

Some of the other pregnant women started laughing too in pure solidarity while a few partners pretended to stare at the ceiling trying desperately to look polite.

One woman in the corner even pointed at Agatha saying, “That happened to me last week,” which unfortunately only made her even more embarrassed.

Agatha groaned, hiding her face in her hands.

“I hate everyone here…”

Rio was practically out of breath from laughing, tears in her eyes while she tried uselessly to recover her composure.

“Sorry… sorry…” she managed between laughs. “Those are really cute atomic bombs.”

Agatha slapped her thigh weakly.

“This is your fault!”

Rio was still laughing.

“Mine?!”

“Yes! I can’t even go to the bathroom properly and I’m basically a balloon full of gas because I’m pregnant with your children!”

That completely destroyed the rest of Rio’s dignity.

She literally had to press her forehead against Agatha’s shoulder while laughing uncontrollably, one hand lightly squeezing her waist.

Natasha had already given up on maintaining any remotely serious atmosphere in that class and simply watched the two of them with crossed arms and an amused smile.

“That’s it,” she commented. “Healthy communication in a relationship.”

Agatha huffed, completely red.

“There’s nothing healthy about farting in front of twenty people.”

“Technically it was three times,” Rio corrected, still laughing.

Agatha slowly turned her face toward her.

“Rio Vidal, I swear to God…”

That only made Natasha laugh too.

Rio raised her hands in surrender, still very obviously trying not to burst out laughing again.

“Alright, alright… sorry.” She kissed Agatha’s shoulder trying to soften it. “They’re still the prettiest farts I’ve ever heard.”

“You’re disturbed.”

“Completely in love, actually.”

After a few more movements and breathing exercises, the class finally came to an end. Some of the pregnant women kept chatting around the room while Natasha organized the equipment and the partners started gathering bags, water bottles, and mats.

Agatha stood up still pouting.

Or at least trying to maintain her indignation.

She adjusted the purple shirt over her belly and walked straight toward the reception desk without looking at Rio, who stayed behind carefully rolling up her exclusive mat along with her bag and water bottle.

Rio watched the omega cross the entire studio with that offended little pout and had to hold back another smile before following after her.

“Don’t be upset with me…”

Agatha crossed her arms immediately.

“I’m sure your new secretary doesn’t fart like that.”

Rio literally had to lower her head for a second trying not to laugh again.

“My love…”

“I’m not upset.” Agatha replied with such an obvious pout that it completely contradicted the statement.

Rio stepped closer slowly, stopping right in front of her before gently holding her face.

“Seriously… I’m sorry.” she said more softly now. “I know it wasn’t very nice. I should’ve controlled myself better.”

Agatha held onto the pout for a few more seconds and failed completely.

“Fine…” she murmured more affectionately. “It was funny.”

Rio smiled immediately before kissing her slowly, one hand stroking her cheek while Agatha sighed, already much less offended.

“I’m going to the bathroom.” Agatha warned.

“Alright.”

She walked down the hallway while Rio stayed standing there watching until she disappeared around the corner near reception.

Then she turned when she heard footsteps approaching.

Natasha was walking toward her holding a clipboard against her chest.

“Class was great today.”

Rio let out a quiet nasal laugh.

“Yeah…” Natasha smiled while watching her expression.

“I’m happy for you. In all these years… I don’t think I’ve ever seen you this happy.”

That made Rio go silent for a moment.

Her eyes automatically drifted toward the direction Agatha had disappeared.

Only then did she answer:

“I know… Agatha feels like a dream. Seriously.”

Natasha watched her with genuine warmth before resting a hand on her shoulder.

“Enjoy it. You deserve to have all of this.”

Rio nodded slowly, but the small smile gradually weakened.

Because deep down there was still a part of her that couldn’t fully relax.

A part that stayed waiting, like all that happiness was too fragile, like at any moment Rita could walk into that perfect bubble and destroy everything Rio had finally managed to build.

The house, the cubs, and Agatha, her entire life now fit inside those things.

◆

The next day Agatha was finishing getting ready for her doctor’s appointment while balancing her phone against the bathroom counter during a video call with Wanda and Jen.

The apartment was still quiet that morning; Rio had left early for a meeting, leaving a sleepy kiss on her forehead before going out.

Agatha adjusted the comfortable cream-colored maternity dress over her already heavy seven-month belly while pinning part of her hair into a messy bun.

“Seriously… she won’t stop praising this secretary.” she complained, frowning at the screen. “Says they’re amazing, competent, organized…”

Jen immediately raised an eyebrow while filing her nails on the call.

“Wait. Rio Vidal actually praised someone?”

“Yes!”

Of course Agatha’s pregnant brain had already blown the whole thing completely out of proportion.

In her head, the “new efficient secretary” was probably some impossible mix between a Vogue supermodel and a billionaire Harvard-trained CEO.

Wanda held back a laugh on the other side of the call.

“Girl, relax. Rio literally worships the ground you walk on.”

“Wanda!”

“It’s true.” Jen agreed without even looking up from her nails. “That woman looks at you like you were grown in a lab specifically for her to desire.”

Agatha huffed, crossing her arms.

“I know… but I hate the fact that someone else is doing this better than me.”

That came out more honestly than she intended.

Because for a long time Agatha had been the one organizing most of Rio’s routine. Meetings. Schedules. Calls. Events.

And now, with the pregnancy getting more advanced, she’d had to step back more and more.

Wanda’s expression softened immediately.

“Hey… you’re creating two human beings. I think you’re allowed to delegate corporate scheduling for a few months.”

Agatha sighed, looking down, her phone vibrated right then with a message from the company driver saying he’d arrived.

“I have to go.” she murmured, grabbing her bag. “I’ve got my appointment today… I’m stopping by the company first to see this new secretary.”

Jen laughed immediately.

“Relax, babe.”

“I am relaxed.” The two of them made the exact same disbelieving expression.

Agatha rolled her eyes.

“Bye.”

She ended the call with a deep breath before grabbing her purse and leaving the apartment.

The hallway of the building was quiet that time of morning, and Agatha walked slowly toward the elevator, feeling the comfortable weight of her belly shift as the babies moved.

As soon as the elevator doors opened on the ground floor, she found the company driver already waiting near the main entrance.

The man immediately opened the car door for her with impeccable politeness.

“Good morning, Mrs. Harkness.”

“Good morning. Thank you for coming.”

“No problem.”

Agatha carefully got into the back seat while he walked around and got into the front.

The car moved smoothly through the busy streets of Manhattan, but Agatha remained restless.

Thinking about that damn secretary.

She stared out the window trying to convince herself she was being ridiculous.

Rio literally slept wrapped around her every night, kissed her belly every morning, and treated Agatha like she was the center of the universe.

And yet, the idea of someone else occupying a space that had once been hers still bothered her.

When the car finally stopped in front of the enormous mirrored building of Vidal Industries, Agatha automatically took a deep breath.

The driver got out first, opening the door for her again.

“Thank you.”

“Have a good morning, ma’am.”

Agatha nodded and walked into the building while employees and security guards immediately greeted her along the way.

Some of them already smiled naturally when they saw her there; her presence in that place had stopped being unusual a long time ago.

She pressed the button for the private elevator, breathing deeply while the doors closed.

The ride up to the penthouse felt way too long, and as the numbers climbed on the panel, her anxiety climbed with them.

The elevator doors opened directly into the executive penthouse of Vidal Industries, and Agatha stepped out carrying her bag while automatically observing the enormous elegant reception area of the presidency floor.

The secretary’s desk was empty, she frowned slightly, Strange.

Rio normally turned every employee into a perfectly organized extension of her own controlled chaos. It was hard to imagine anyone leaving that area messy.

But it didn’t take long for the reason to appear.

A young man with curly hair came practically running down the hallway carrying an absurd stack of documents, folders, and a tablet balanced very precariously in his arms.

His expression was pure panic.

“Good morning!” he stammered awkwardly while dumping everything onto the desk, nearly knocking half of it over in the process.

Agatha had to hold back her laughter immediately.

“Good morning…”

The boy started organizing the papers at a desperate speed.

“Where is the secr—”

“I’m the new secretary!” he answered way too quickly, clearly nervous. “Good morning… I’m still getting used to the routine, I’m so sorry…”

His desk was a complete disaster.

Post-its, crooked reports, scattered pens, and a half-empty coffee cup that had clearly been abandoned there for hours, Agatha almost laughed at the entire scene.

This was the complete opposite of what she’d imagined.

“Agatha.” she said more gently. “I’m Rio Vidal’s girlfriend.”

The boy completely froze, his eyes widening to an almost comical degree.

“Oh my God.”

Absolute panic took over him.

“I didn’t receive you the way she instructed me to!” he blurted out horrified. “She said every guest has to be greeted with coffee or water, oh my God I’m going to get fired…”

“Relax.” Agatha immediately tried to reassure him. “It’s fine, seriously—”

But every effort became useless the moment another voice appeared behind her.

“Kaplan. Where were you?”

Billy turned pale.

“Sorry, Mrs. Vidal, I… I…”

“Rio, don’t terrorize the poor boy.” Agatha interrupted immediately with an amused little smile.

The change in Rio was instant.

She dropped the cold corporate posture the second she properly saw Agatha, walking toward her without hesitation to kiss her cheek delicately before wrapping an arm around her shoulders and pulling her close against her body.

“You got lucky, Billy.” she commented calmly. “I’m always in a good mood when she’s around.”



Billy looked even more terrified now. Rio pulled the car keys from the pocket of her blazer and tossed them to him without warning. The poor boy almost dropped them.

“When you finish scanning those reports, take my car in for servicing.”

“But I—”

“And I don’t want to see a single scratch.”

Billy nodded so fast Agatha was sure he’d agree to pilot a plane if Rio ordered him to.

Then Rio simply turned, leading Agatha with her toward the private elevator. The moment the doors closed behind them, Agatha started laughing.

“My God, you’re horrible.”

Rio raised an innocent eyebrow.

“The world is already too easy when you’re a beta man. I need to give him challenges.”

Agatha burst into an incredulous laugh.

“You’re torturing the poor boy.”

“Well… he’s promising.” Rio replied calmly. “I really do praise him to you.”

That made Agatha feel an immediate stab of embarrassment over the absurd jealousy she’d created in her own head.

Because the “efficient new secretary” was literally a terrified college student trying to survive Rio Vidal.

“And anyway…” Rio continued while pressing the button for her office floor. “I’m doing his father a favor.”

Agatha tilted her head curiously.

“What do you mean?”

“Jeff Kaplan. His father is one of my businessmen,” she explained calmly. “He wants his son in the corporate world, but he doesn’t want to make things easy.”

A small crooked smile appeared on her face.

“Well… I’m definitely not making things easy.”

Agatha started laughing again imagining that boy’s daily despair.

“You’re scary.”

Rio slowly turned her face toward her.

“And yet you got pregnant by me twice.”

Agatha rolled her eyes immediately while Rio smiled, entirely pleased with herself.

“In my defense… I didn’t know it was you,” Agatha shot back immediately.

Rio slowly turned toward her as they walked through the elegant hallway of the penthouse floor.

“And if you had known? Would you still have gotten pregnant?”

Agatha made a thoughtful little pout as if she were genuinely considering the question with extreme seriousness.

“Mm… no idea.”

Rio let out a quiet laugh through her nose before gently pulling her by the waist and kissing her again. A quick, affectionate kiss, enough to draw an involuntary little giggle from Agatha.

The two continued together toward the company’s private exit, where the car was already waiting with the rear door open. The driver immediately straightened his posture when he saw them approaching.

Rio helped Agatha inside first, carefully holding her hand while the omega settled into the back seat. Only then did she climb in beside her, and the driver closed the door before heading toward the hospital for the appointment.

Agatha crossed her arms, still trying to maintain a fake indignation.

“Why didn’t you tell me your new secretary was a clumsy boy who’s completely terrified?”

Rio smiled immediately.

“Because I think your jealousy is cute.”

“I was not jealous.”

“Sure.”

“You’re the jealous one here.”

“At least I admit it,” Rio replied calmly.

Agatha huffed, unable to hide the small smile while absentmindedly rubbing her belly over the dress.

The car moved smoothly through the busy streets of Manhattan while Rio relaxed slightly against the seat beside her.

Then the alpha’s expression shifted subtly.

“I kept reading the diary.”

Agatha immediately turned toward her.

“And?” Rio let out a quiet sigh before answering.

“It just ends out of nowhere.”

Her fingers absentmindedly played over Agatha’s belly as she spoke.

“Claire and Rita were having some kind of disagreement… something happened around the time Claire was pregnant with my mother. They seemed to be fighting about something important.”

Agatha watched her face silently.

“And then?”

“Then nothing,” Rio answered more quietly. “The pages just stop. I think someone tore them out.”

That made Agatha’s chest tighten immediately.

Because the more they discovered about that family, the worse it seemed to become.

“I’m sorry…”

Rio shook her head slowly.

“It’s okay,” she murmured. “I think things are going to move faster now that the detective managed to make progress on part of the investigation.”

Agatha nodded softly.

Rio slid her hand over her belly again, fingers absently stroking the fabric of the dress.

“They’re quiet…”

Agatha smiled faintly.

“They’re sleeping.”

Rio immediately feigned indignation.

“At this hour? During business hours?”

Agatha let out a tired little laugh.

Rio then leaned down slowly, kissing her belly over the dress, completely indifferent to the fact they were inside a corporate car.

“They’re allowed,” she declared solemnly. Agatha affectionately stroked her dark hair.

“Yes, they are.”

The car stopped smoothly in front of the hospital, and before the driver could even fully get out, Rio was already opening the door for Agatha.

She carefully held the omega’s hand while helping her out, immediately resting her palm against the small of her back.

Agatha had already realized Rio did that without even thinking anymore.

The two walked together through the wide hospital entrance while staff and nurses discreetly greeted Rio along the way.

The clean scent of antiseptic mixed with cold air conditioning hit Agatha the moment they stepped inside.

Rio carried her bag over one shoulder while keeping her other hand against her lower back, guiding her slowly toward the private elevator.

“You’re walking slower today,” she commented quietly while watching her.

“Because your children are crushing my internal organs.”

Rio smiled immediately.

“Our children.”

Agatha rolled her eyes, but the small smile appeared anyway.

The elevator rose in silence to the obstetrics floor.

As soon as the doors opened, they found Lorna already at the reception desk talking to a nurse while holding a clipboard.

The doctor turned toward them the moment she saw the two and smiled.

“Right on time.”

Her gaze immediately traveled over Agatha from head to toe, assessing her posture, her belly, even the way she walked.

Agatha noticed instantly.

“Is it obvious?” she asked with a laugh.

Lorna raised an amused eyebrow.

“The fact your belly has dropped dramatically? Yes. These babies want to be born soon.”

That made Agatha hold her breath for a second before automatically looking down at her own belly.

“I can’t believe it’s so close…”

Rio immediately leaned in and kissed her temple delicately.

“I can,” she murmured softly.

Lorna then motioned for them to follow her into one of the exam rooms. As soon as they entered, she handed a hospital gown to Agatha.

“Put this on for me while I prepare the ultrasound.”

Agatha nodded, taking the clothes while Rio automatically walked with her to the room’s private bathroom, closing the door behind them.

That had already become routine between them. Silently, naturally, Rio helped before Agatha even had to ask.

Because now her belly was big enough to make small everyday things difficult. And Rio seemed strangely happy taking care of every single one of them.

She approached slowly, helping Agatha take off the dress. Agatha raised her arms and Rio carefully pulled the fabric upward, sliding the piece over her body until she was left only in her bra and panties.

Rio’s eyes inevitably wandered over her skin for a moment.

The round belly, her softer curves, the small pink stretch marks scattered across her hips.

Everything about her seemed absurdly beautiful to Rio.

“Thank you…” Agatha whispered.

Rio smiled immediately, in that small sincere way that only appeared around her.

“I like taking care of you.”

Agatha let out a quiet little laugh before resting one hand against the counter.

“Well… the next thing you’re doing today when we get home is shaving me in the shower, so I’m glad we skipped past the embarrassment phase quickly.”

Rio immediately burst into surprised laughter. It was unbelievable how Agatha could be funny even in the most embarrassing moments.

And every time she made Rio laugh like that, genuinely, the alpha silently wondered where this woman had been her entire life, because it seemed impossible that a world before Agatha had ever existed.

Rio then carefully helped her into the hospital gown, adjusting the fabric lovingly over her belly before crouching down to gently pull Agatha’s panties down her legs.

When she stood again, she was holding the panties between her teeth with the most absurdly smug expression possible. Agatha’s eyes widened immediately before she started laughing.

“You’re ridiculous.”

Rio dramatically removed the panties from her mouth.

“Only with you,” she answered calmly. “Outside of that, I terrorize young incompetent secretaries.”

Agatha almost lost her breath laughing while Rio finally handed the panties back for her to put in her bag.

The two left the bathroom moments later, and Rio was still carrying Agatha’s bag while carefully guiding her toward the examination bed in the center of the room.

The pale hospital gown made her enormous belly completely obvious now, round and low, the fabric barely able to hide the shape of the two babies settled inside.

Lorna was already organizing the ultrasound machine beside the bed when she looked up at them.

“Alright, let’s see how these little bunnies are doing today.”

Agatha smiled automatically at the nickname while slowly settling onto the bed. Rio immediately stayed beside her, helping her climb up and adjusting a pillow behind her back before the doctor even had to ask.

Lorna pulled on gloves while beginning the standard prenatal procedures.

First she checked Agatha’s blood pressure, watching the numbers carefully.

“Blood pressure looks great.”

Then she checked heart rate, oxygen levels, and asked a few questions about Braxton Hicks contractions, sleep, swelling, and fetal movement.

“They’re still moving a lot?” she asked while writing in the chart.

“Mostly at night,” Agatha answered tiredly. “I think they both hate my sleep schedule.”

Rio smirked faintly.

“Just like their mother.”

Lorna completely ignored the jab and asked Agatha to lie back more comfortably for the abdominal ultrasound. Then she lifted the gown above her belly and spread the warm gel across the stretched skin.

Rio immediately took her hand.

A few seconds later the monitor turned on, and the rapid sound of two heartbeats instantly filled the room.

Agatha let out an automatic emotional sigh every single time she heard that.

Even after months.

And Rio clearly never got used to it either.

Her eyes stayed glued to the screen while Lorna carefully moved the transducer across Agatha’s belly.

“Both of them still look great,” she commented while observing the images. “They’ve grown a lot since the last appointment.”

She began carefully measuring abdominal circumference, femur length, head diameter, and the babies’ positions while explaining each detail.

“The first baby is approximately two kilos one hundred now… and the second is a little smaller, but perfectly within the expected range for twins.”

Rio watched everything with absurd attention.

“That’s good?” she immediately asked.

Lorna smiled.

“Very good. They’ve gained quite a bit of weight these last few weeks.”

She continued the exam, checking umbilical cord flow, placenta, and amniotic fluid levels before pausing again on one of the chest images.

“The lungs are very well developed for the gestational age,” she explained calmly. “But they still need a little more time before the ideal birth.”

Agatha nodded, breathing more easily.

“So they’re not being born right now?”

“Doesn’t look like it,” Lorna replied. “But they’re definitely preparing. Your belly has dropped a lot and your body has already started some adaptations.”

Rio immediately became more alert.

“What kind of adaptations?”

Lorna shot her an amused look.

“Relax, Vidal.”

Even then Rio clearly did not relax at all.

The doctor continued observing the babies for a few more minutes until she got an image where one of them literally seemed to be shoving the other.

“Oh… there it is. The sibling fights already started.”

Agatha started laughing while Rio looked absurdly fascinated.

“They’re actually fighting?”

“They’re fighting over space,” Lorna corrected, amused. “The uterus is getting cramped for two.”

After the abdominal ultrasound, she helped Agatha sit up slowly again before continuing the physical exams. She checked swelling in her legs, measured uterine size, and listened once more to the babies’ heartbeats in different positions.

Then her expression became slightly more serious.

“I’m going to need to do a transvaginal exam today too, just to evaluate your cervix and risk of premature labor, alright?”

Agatha nodded.

“That’s fine.”

Rio immediately took her hand again while Lorna prepared the equipment.

The doctor asked Agatha to reposition herself on the bed and partially cover her legs with the hospital sheet. The exam was done carefully and professionally while Lorna observed the cervical images on the monitor.

The silence in the room lasted a few minutes. Rio was clearly tense again.

“And?”

Lorna finished the measurements before removing the device and taking off her gloves.

“Cervix is preserved. No significant dilation,” she explained calmly. “We still have a little more time before these two decide to show up.”

Rio slowly let out the breath she’d apparently been holding for minutes.

Agatha looked at her, laughing softly.

“You get more nervous than I do.”

“Because you didn’t see the size of their heads on that screen,” Rio shot back immediately.

Lorna started laughing while organizing the exams.

“Honestly? That’s a fair comment.”

Lorna finished writing the last information in the chart while Agatha adjusted the gown over her body again. Rio remained beside her, still absentmindedly holding the omega’s hand while watching the doctor’s every movement with excessive attention.

The obstetrician analyzed the exams for a few more seconds before looking up.

“Overall, everything seems fine.”

Agatha immediately let out a slow breath.

But Lorna continued:

“However… your cervix showed a few small changes that I want to investigate further.”

Rio stiffened instantly.

“What kind of changes?”

Lorna kept her tone calm.

“Nothing alarming for now. It could simply be the effect of a twin pregnancy and the babies’ weight… but some changes may indicate Agatha’s blood sugar is a little elevated.”

Agatha frowned slightly.

“Gestational diabetes?”

“Maybe,” Lorna answered honestly. “I can’t say for sure without tests, but I want to investigate before it becomes a bigger issue.”

Rio immediately went into full alert mode.

“Is that dangerous?”

“If it’s controlled early? Usually not,” she explained calmly while writing a few more things down. “But we need to monitor it because twin pregnancies naturally increase some metabolic risks.”

Agatha sighed, rubbing her belly.

“So my absurd cravings for sweets finally caught up to me.”

Lorna smiled.

“It’s not your fault. Placental hormones can interfere quite a bit with insulin resistance, especially in twin pregnancies.”

Rio already looked mentally prepared to eliminate every existing form of sugar within a fifty-kilometer radius.

Agatha noticed immediately.

“Don’t even think about it.”

“I didn’t say anything.”

“You literally made the face of a woman about to ban desserts.”

Rio crossed her arms with dignity.

“I’m thinking about my children’s health.”

“Our children like apple pie.”

“Our children also enjoy crushing your internal organs and farting during childbirth class. Not everything they like is healthy.”

Lorna had to lower her head to hide a laugh while printing the test requests.

“I’m going to order a glucose tolerance test and some additional blood work. Depending on the results, we may only need dietary adjustments and closer monitoring.”

Rio nodded immediately.

“We’re doing it today.”

“Rio—”

“Today,” she repeated, far too calmly.

Agatha dramatically rolled her eyes.

“She’s going to put me under house arrest with green leaves.”

Rio leaned down, kissing her forehead.

“I’m not taking away your sweets… I’m just supervising your crimes.”

Agatha huffed while Lorna finally handed over the test forms.

“I also want you to reduce physical effort over the next few weeks,” the doctor added while looking directly at Agatha. “No climbing on furniture, carrying boxes, or doing reckless things without supervision.”

Rio immediately turned her head slowly toward Agatha.

Absolute silence.

Agatha avoided eye contact instantly.

“You climbed on furniture again?”

“Technically—”

“Agatha.”

“It was just a small picture frame!”

Rio closed her eyes, breathing deeply while Lorna started laughing without the slightest sympathy for her.

“I’m definitely moving your appointments up just so I can keep following this soap opera in person.”

Rio slowly crossed her arms while still staring at Agatha with that firm look she always used whenever she decided something.

“The soap opera is getting canceled,” she declared calmly. “Because the heroine isn’t climbing on anything anymore.”

Agatha let out a tired little laugh while Lorna simply nodded, satisfied.

“Very good,” the doctor commented before leaving the room to request the tests.

As soon as they were alone again, Agatha sighed and adjusted herself better on the bed, resting her hands around her heavy belly.

“Sorry…” she murmured quietly. “I just wanted to hang up the bunny painting already.”

Rio’s serious expression softened immediately. She walked toward her slowly and carefully held her face before kissing her forehead for a long moment.

“My love… you just have to ask me.”

Agatha looked away briefly.

“I know. It’s just… sometimes I hate feeling like I can’t do things on my own.”

Something inside Rio tightened instantly.

She moved closer between Agatha’s legs, keeping one hand stroking the side of her belly over the hospital gown.

“You are not useless,” she said firmly. “Not even close.”

Agatha took a deep breath while listening.

“You are literally doing the most important job of all right now. You’re growing our babies. Your body is working all the time.”

Her voice softened even more.

“And I need you to be careful because I love the three of you more than anything.”

Agatha’s eyes immediately grew watery. The hormones definitely weren’t helping.

“You’re right…”

“I always am,” Rio answered immediately.

Agatha rolled her eyes.

“Arrogant.”

Rio smirked faintly.

“And you will never be useless. Ever.”

Then she kissed her again, slowly this time, just to calm her down.

A few minutes later, the door opened again and Lorna returned carrying a tray with tubes, syringes, and a few small transparent bottles containing a sugary liquid.

Agatha frowned immediately.

“That looks disgusting.”

Lorna let out a small laugh.

“Glucose tolerance test. We’re going to measure how your body reacts to glucose.”

Rio was already helping Agatha sit more comfortably on the bed while the doctor organized the materials.

“First we draw blood fasting, then you drink the glucose solution and we repeat the blood draws at specific intervals.”

Agatha picked up the little bottle suspiciously.

“This looks like cough syrup.”

“Honestly? It doesn’t taste much better,” Lorna admitted.

Rio immediately grabbed a bottle of water to leave beside her while absentmindedly stroking the back of her neck.

“You can do it.”

Agatha looked at her dramatically.

“If I throw up, it’s going to be on you.”

“That’s fine.”

“You say that now.”

Lorna prepared the first blood draw while Rio held her hand the entire time. Agatha made the slightest grimace when the needle went in, but stayed quiet.

Then came the worst part.

The drink was absurdly sweet.

Agatha took one sip and immediately made a horrified face.

“Oh my God.”

Rio started laughing instantly.

“That bad?”

“It tastes like someone melted twenty lollipops into a bottle.”

Even while complaining, she finished drinking it under Lorna’s satisfied gaze.

“Very good. Now we wait a little before the next draw.”

Lorna left the room, and Rio immediately pulled one of the reclining armchairs closer to the bed and sat beside her, opening her arms without even needing to ask.

Agatha went to her without thinking and settled onto Rio’s lap, leaning against her with a tired sigh while Rio absentmindedly kissed her hair.

“After this I deserve pie.”

“After this you deserve vegetables,” Rio answered calmly.

Agatha closed her eyes in outrage.

“Cruel.”

After hours between blood draws, absurdly sweet drinks, and far too much time sitting in that room, Agatha was completely miserable now.

The nausea came in waves.

Her belly felt even bigger against her exhausted body, and she had completely given up on maintaining any remaining dignity.

She was practically curled up in Rio’s arms in the large chair beside the bed, her head resting on the alpha’s shoulder while Rio slowly rubbed her back.

Trying to ease the nausea.

“I never want to see sugar again,” Agatha murmured miserably.

Rio kissed her hair.

“That” coming from the woman who almost cried when I hid the donuts last week.”

“I was vulnerable.”

Rio smiled softly while continuing to stroke her back.

The door opened again a few minutes later and Lorna walked in, observing the two of them. Her eyes immediately dropped to the empty little bottles on the tray.

“I’m genuinely impressed you managed to drink all of that without throwing up.”

Agatha barely lifted her face from Rio’s shoulder.

“Can we please get this over with? Because I feel horrible.”

Lorna nodded immediately.

“Last draw. I’ll call the nurse.” As soon as she left, Rio slowly kissed Agatha’s temple again.

“You’re doing really well.”

“Liar… I look like a ghost.”

“You look beautiful.”

Agatha didn’t even have the energy to argue back.

A few minutes later, Lorna returned with a nurse carrying the materials for the final blood draw. Agatha sighed deeply while tiredly extending her arm.

Rio still held her in her lap, protecting her while watching everything.

The nurse tied the tourniquet around Agatha’s arm and started searching for the vein while calmly explaining the procedure. Agatha made a small grimace of discomfort, and that was enough.

The moment the needle pierced her skin, something instinctive tore through Rio in an almost animalistic way.

An immediate and violent impulse.

Her body stiffened instantly and her jaw tightened. For one entire second Rio felt the urge to literally rip that needle out of Agatha’s arm.

Even rationally knowing it was necessary, even knowing it was just a simple exam and still, the protective instinct crushed everything else.

Lorna noticed the shift in her posture immediately, because Rio slowly lowered her head, hiding her face against Agatha’s shoulder while trying to control herself.

Her warm breath hit the omega’s skin, and Agatha felt it.

The small sound trapped in Rio’s chest, a growl forcibly held back, Agatha slightly turned her face in surprise before understanding.

Then an absurdly loving expression crossed her face despite the nausea.

“Rio…” she whispered softly while stroking her dark hair.

The alpha only tightened her arms further around her waist while the nurse finished the draw as quickly as possible.

“Sorry…” Rio murmured against her skin. “I hate this.”

Lorna was clearly fighting not to smile while organizing the blood tubes.

“You do know she’s survived things way worse than a blood draw, right?”

“Doesn’t matter.” Rio answered immediately without even lifting her face.

Agatha let out a tired little laugh despite feeling sick.

“My God… you really turned into that kind of overprotective alpha.”

“Yes, and I have no intention of getting better.”

The nurse finished the blood draw a few moments later, gently placing the bandage on Agatha’s arm before stepping away. The second the needle came out, the omega’s body finally seemed to relax a little.

She closed her eyes tiredly against Rio’s chest, she immediately nestled her better in her arms, sliding her hand slowly along her back while watching the nurse leave the room with the test tubes.

Lorna finished organizing the clipboard before looking at them more softly.

“It’s going to be okay,” she assured calmly. “As soon as the results come out, I’ll send everything to you, Rio.”

Rio nodded immediately.

“Thank you.” Lorna smiled faintly before walking toward the door.

“I’ll give you two a few minutes to recover.” Then she quietly left the room.

Agatha stayed with her eyes closed, breathing deeply against Rio’s body while the alpha continued stroking her hair.

“Still nauseous?” she asked softly. Agatha nodded slightly.

“A little…”

Rio slowly kissed her forehead.

“We’re going home soon.” Agatha stayed quiet for a few more seconds before slowly opening her eyes.

“Can you take me to the bathroom?”

Rio didn’t even answer verbally. She simply slid one arm under Agatha’s legs and the other around her back, lifting her into her arms effortlessly.

The omega automatically let out a tired little sigh, clinging to her while Rio carried her to the private bathroom in the room.

As soon as she entered, she carefully placed Agatha back on the floor, slowly, as if she were made of glass.

Agatha thanked her quietly before slowly walking to the toilet while Rio remained leaning against the wall in front of her, watching every little movement.

The bathroom was silent except for the low hum of the air conditioner.

Agatha looked exhausted now.

She leaned part of her weight against the sink in front of her while sitting there, still dizzy from the exam and the absurd amount of glucose.

Rio watched in silence, feeling her chest tighten slowly, she hated seeing Agatha like that, fragile, pale, exhausted, and even rationally knowing it was part of the pregnancy, she still felt guilty, as if she were demanding too much from Agatha’s body.

As if the babies were slowly consuming her while Rio simply watched without being able to stop it, even half-asleep, Agatha noticed her silence.

“Hey…” she murmured softly, looking at Rio through the mirror’s reflection. “Don’t make that face.”

Rio sighed, leaning her head back against the wall.

“I hate when you feel sick.”

Agatha slowly washed her hands before giving her a small tired smile.

“It’s worth it.” That almost destroyed Rio from the inside.

She immediately walked over to help Agatha again, holding her waist while they slowly left the bathroom.

She helped Agatha get dressed quickly, the sleepiness was clearly winning now.

Agatha walked softer, slower, leaning almost all her weight against Rio, when they left the hospital, Rio’s car was already waiting at the entrance.

The driver had probably brought the vehicle around after the inspection while they were finishing the exams.

Rio opened both the passenger and driver doors and immediately helped Agatha inside. The omega practically collapsed into the comfortable seat, letting out a long sigh before closing her eyes.

The alpha carefully fastened the seatbelt around her, brushed a few strands of hair away from her face, and only then walked around and got into the driver’s seat.

City traffic moved slowly outside while the inside of the car stayed warm and quiet.

Agatha fell asleep instantly, Rio looked at her several times during the drive.

Watching her calm breathing, the round belly rising and falling slowly beneath the dress, the occasional little movements from the babies even while she slept.

And then, on instinct, Rio stretched her free hand toward Agatha’s thigh, touching her just to make sure she was there.

The car slowly turned onto their street while Rio kept one hand on the wheel and the other still close to Agatha’s leg. The light rain beginning to fall made the asphalt shine beneath the city lights.

But then she slowed down, her expression changing immediately, Red and blue lights flashed farther ahead, Police cars, a lot of them.

Rio frowned as every instinct inside her went on alert, Agatha slowly woke up with the sudden change in the car’s rhythm and the reflection of the sirens cutting through the windows.

She blinked in confusion.

“What’s happening…?” 

Rio kept staring straight ahead for a second far too long.

“Wait here,” she said immediately.

Rio turned off the car and got out almost instantly. The door slammed hard behind her as she quickly crossed the street toward the building.

All she could see was the entrance surrounded by police officers, and then she saw Lilia. 

The landlady was standing near the main door talking to one of the officers, clearly shaken, her usually impeccable face looked pale now. The moment she saw Rio approaching, her expression changed immediately.

Rio felt her stomach drop.

“Lilia,” she called firmly. “What happened?”

Lilia turned toward her, startled, and for one full second Rio knew the answer would be horrible.

“They broke into Agatha’s apartment.”

Afterword
